code: d <- h5ad_as_daf("path/to/file.h5ad")

title: AnnData interop
code:
fixture <- system.file("extdata", "small_test.h5ad", package = "dafr") if (file.exists(fixture) && requireNamespace("hdf5r", quietly = TRUE)) {     d <- h5ad_as_daf(fixture)     cat("loaded:", daf_name(d), "
")     cat("n_obs:", length(axis_entries(d, "obs")), "
")     cat("n_vars:", length(axis_entries(d, "var")), "
") } #> loaded: small_test  #> n_obs: 50  #> n_vars: 20

title: AnnData interop
text: h5ad_as_daf() (daf_as_h5ad() direction) cover: /X dense sparse (csr_matrix, csc_matrix) - sparse round-tripped via Matrix::sparseMatrix. /obs /var columns: plain datasets categorical groups (read factor, ordered ). /layers - dense layers become extra obs x var matrices keyed layer name. /obsm /varm dense matrices - placed synthetic axis named obsm_<name>_dim / varm_<name>_dim. /uns scalars (flat nested; nested keys flattened _). Things still translated (escalated via unsupported_handler, default WARN_HANDLER, skipped): /obsp /varp (square per-pair matrices obs/var). /raw (legacy raw counts slot). Sparse layers / obsm / varm entries; dense forms read. Group-valued obs/var columns categorical. strict round-trip matters, pass unsupported_handler = ERROR_HANDLER unhandled entries hard-fail instead dropped silently.

title: Getting Started with dafr
text: Daf (Data Axes Format) typed, multi-axis container scientific data. daf holds: Axes - named lists entries (e.g. cell, gene, donor). Scalars - single values (e.g. organism = "human"). Vectors - one value per entry chosen axis. Matrices - one value per pair entries two chosen axes. Think AnnData generalised number axes, typed scalars, -disk persistence several formats, query DSL composing reads.

title: Getting Started with dafr
text: description(d) produces detailed dump axes, vectors, matrices, scalars, useful inspecting unfamiliar dafs:
code: d <- memory_daf(name = "demo") add_axis(d, "cell", c("c1", "c2", "c3")) add_axis(d, "gene", c("g1", "g2")) set_scalar(d, "organism", "human") set_vector(d, "cell", "donor", c("A", "B", "A")) set_matrix(d, "cell", "gene", "UMIs",            matrix(1:6, nrow = 3, ncol = 2)) print(d) #> <dafr::MemoryDaf> #>  @ name                  : chr "demo" #>  @ internal              :<environment: 0x5605c9438bc0>  #>  @ cache                 :<environment: 0x5605c9475b90>  #>  @ axis_version_counter  :<environment: 0x5605c9479fb8>  #>  @ vector_version_counter:<environment: 0x5605c9479ce0>  #>  @ matrix_version_counter:<environment: 0x5605c9479a08> cat(description(d)) #> name: demo #> type: MemoryDaf #> scalars: #>   organism: "human" #> axes: #>   cell: 3 entries #>   gene: 2 entries #> vectors: #>   cell: #>     donor #> matrices: #>   cell,gene: #>     UMIs #>   gene,cell: #>     UMIs

title: Getting Started with dafr
text: scalars_set(d), vectors_set(d, axis), matrices_set(d, , b) list property names present corresponding container.
code: get_scalar(d, "organism") #> [1] "human" get_vector(d, "cell", "donor") #>  c1  c2  c3  #> "A" "B" "A" get_matrix(d, "cell", "gene", "UMIs") #>    g1 g2 #> c1  1  4 #> c2  2  5 #> c3  3  6

title: Getting Started with dafr
text: Queries let compose reads. Two equivalent forms - string DSL pipe-chain builders - share execution engine: See vignette("queries", package = "dafr") practical tour vignette("query-dsl-reference", package = "dafr") full operator grammar.
code: d[". organism"] #> [1] "human" d[Axis("cell") |> LookupVector("donor")] #>  c1  c2  c3  #> "A" "B" "A" # Mean UMI count per cell (reduce across the gene axis): d["@ cell @ gene :: UMIs >| Mean"] #>  c1  c2  c3  #> 2.5 3.5 4.5

title: Getting Started with dafr
text: get_dataframe() materialises axis-keyed slice tibble: dplyr backend lets treat axis tbl pipe verbs: See vignette("dplyr", package = "dafr") supported verbs write-back semantics.
code: get_dataframe(d, "cell") #>    donor #> c1     A #> c2     B #> c3     A library(dplyr, warn.conflicts = FALSE) tbl(d, "cell") |>     group_by(donor) |>     summarise(n = n()) #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>   donor     n #>   <chr> <int> #> 1 A         2 #> 2 B         1

title: Getting Started with dafr
text: daf can persisted three -disk shapes - pick one matches data needs travel. zarr_daf() (directory .daf.zarr.zip) http_daf() (read-HTTP) round-trip data; see vignette("zarr", package = "dafr").
code: # files_daf: one file per property, mmap-backed reads. path <- tempfile("dafr-") fd <- files_daf(path, mode = "w+", name = "persisted") copy_all(fd, d)  # destination first, then source list.files(path, recursive = TRUE) #>  [1] "axes/cell.txt"                "axes/gene.txt"                #>  [3] "axes/metadata.json"           "daf.json"                     #>  [5] "matrices/cell/gene/UMIs.data" "matrices/cell/gene/UMIs.json" #>  [7] "matrices/gene/cell/UMIs.data" "matrices/gene/cell/UMIs.json" #>  [9] "metadata.zip"                 "scalars/organism.json"        #> [11] "vectors/cell/donor.json"      "vectors/cell/donor.txt" # Reopen read-only: fd2 <- files_daf(path, mode = "r") get_scalar(fd2, "organism") #> [1] "human" get_matrix(fd2, "cell", "gene", "UMIs") #>    g1 g2 #> c1  1  4 #> c2  2  5 #> c3  3  6

title: Getting Started with dafr

Zarr & HTTP storage - vignette("zarr", package = "dafr"). Native performance notes - vignette("native-performance", package = "dafr"). Larger building blocks - chains (chain_reader), views (viewer, VIEW_ALL_DATA), contracts (Contract, verify_input), computations (computation, adapter) detailed help pages reference.

title: dplyr backend
text: filter, select, mutate, arrange, summarise, group_by, distinct, pull work ’d expect. collect() materializes tbl tibble.
code: tbl(d, "cell") |>     filter(!is.na(donor)) |>     group_by(donor) |>     summarise(n = n()) |>     arrange(desc(n)) #> <daf_axis_tbl>  axis: donor  [95 rows] #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   name      n #>   <chr> <int> #> 1 N104     34 #> 2 N99      27 #> 3 N96      21 #> 4 N118     20 #> 5 N113     19 #> 6 N159     19

title: dplyr backend
text: mutate() stores computed columns memory; touch daf. Persisting vectors axis requires explicit compute(vectors = ...) — dafr never silently writes daf. daf backend reuses dplyr::compute() (extra vectors = ... argument) rather introducing new generic, doesn’t shadow dplyr::compute() dbplyr users. compute() errors tbl filtered (partial row mask) — write back partial vector. permuted--full row mask (e.g. arrange()) un-permuted axis order writing.
code: tbl(d, "cell") |>     mutate(log_umis = log10(n_umis)) |>     compute(vectors = "log_umis")

what: Grouping that looks up an axis
title: dplyr backend
text: grouping variable names existing axis daf, result summarise() daf_axis_tbl keyed axis. can keep piping dplyr verbs, later compute() derived columns back vectors axis. grouping variable just vector (axis), summarise() returns plain tibble.
code: # "donor" is an axis; the summarise result is itself tbl(d, "donor")-like. tbl(d, "cell") |>     group_by(donor) |>     summarise(mean_umis = mean(n_umis)) |>     compute(vectors = "mean_umis")  # persists on the donor axis

title: dplyr backend
text: Beyond core set , backend also supports slice slice_head / slice_tail / slice_min / slice_max / slice_sample family; rename relocate; count / tally / add_count / add_tally; transmute reframe. Inside mutate() summarise(), common dplyr helpers work via delegation: Window functions: lag, lead, cumsum, row_number, min_rank, dense_rank, ntile, percent_rank. Scalar helpers: if_else, case_when, coalesce, n_distinct, first, last, nth. across((.numeric), mean)-style column-wise ops. Tidyselect helpers select(): starts_with, contains, matches, . .= ... (dplyr 1.1+) works filter() / mutate() / summarise(); single-axis .summarise() ties back way group_by . mutate(.keep = "none") respected.

title: dplyr backend
text: Matrices. Use Axis(...) |> LookupMatrix(...) query DSL. Joins (inner_join, left_join, right_join, full_join, semi_join, anti_join, cross_join, nest_join). daf_axis_tbl meaningful join semantics another table v1. Calling produces helpful error pointing dplyr::collect(): Set operations (union, union_all, intersect, setdiff) — story. rowwise() — collect rewrite vectorized mutate(). DSL pushdown filter / summarise. Verbs currently materialize via get_vector, cheap memory daf optimal large FilesDaf selective filters.
code: tbl(d, "cell") |> left_join(other) #> Error: `left_join()` is not supported on a daf_axis_tbl. #> Use `dplyr::collect()` to materialize a tibble first, then apply this verb.

title: FilesDaf on-disk format
text: FilesDaf directory-based storage format Daf data model. directory tree holds scalars, axes, vectors, matrices individual files, self-describing, trivially machine-readable form. every property lives file, standard OS tools (ls, wc, od, make) can inspect manipulate data without special library. trade-store directory rather single archive; shipping requires zip, tar, similar. binary data stored contiguous sequence fixed-size elements little-endian byte order headers alignment padding (files_format.jl:111–113). String data stored one entry per line, terminated \n regardless host OS (files_format.jl:115–117). Dense matrices stored column-major (Fortran / Julia-native) layout (files_format.jl:57–58, files_format.jl:111–113).

title: FilesDaf on-disk format
text: valid FilesDaf root directory contains exactly following structure (files_format.jl:18–19, files_format.jl:222–226): four subdirectories created unconditionally store initialised (files_format.jl:222–226). directory lacks daf.json rejected “daf directory” (files_format.jl:230–231). Unknown files subdirectories root subdirectory silently ignored reader; discovery suffix (.json scalars property descriptors, .txt axes), unrecognised files cause error (files_format.jl:959–972).
code: <root>/ ├── daf.json                    # required — format sentinel and version ├── scalars/                    # required — scalar properties ├── axes/                       # required — axis entry lists ├── vectors/                    # required — per-axis vector properties └── matrices/                   # required — per-axis-pair matrix properties

title: FilesDaf on-disk format
text: daf.json UTF-8 JSON object single required field: defined version [1, 0] (files_format.jl:25, MAJOR_VERSION = 1, MINOR_VERSION = 0, files_format.jl:155–165). Compatibility rule (files_format.jl:239–245): reader must reject store whose major version differs one knows (1), must reject store whose minor version greater maximum supports (0). reader may open stores lower minor version maximum. Example: (Written trailing \n Julia implementation — files_format.jl:220 — newline semantically significant JSON parser.) top-level keys defined version [1,0]. Unknown keys tolerated readers (standard JSON forward-compatibility practice), explicitly enforced reference implementation.
code: {"version":[1,0]}

title: FilesDaf on-disk format
text: scalar property stored single JSON file: JSON object two required fields (files_format.jl:28–29, files_format.jl:272–288, files_format.jl:303–319): Canonical type strings — writer-canonical names, produced Julia’s "$(type)": Reading case-insensitive known aliases. reader files_format.jl:309 accepts "String" "string", DTYPE_BY_NAME dispatch table (operations.jl:216–242) maps capitalised ("Float32", "Int32", …) lowercase ("float32", "int32", …) variants type. Conforming readers must accept casings. Numeric values stored JSON numbers. Bool stored 0/1 (JSON integers). Float32 / Float64 stored JSON floating-point numbers. Integers stored JSON integers. Float32 precision JSON. writer emit "Float64" canonical type IEEE 754 binary64 scalars. "Float32" scalars written JSON.Writer.print formatted 7 significant decimal digits may round-trip lossily depending JSON library; imprecision affects scalars stored "Float32". Binary payloads (vector .data, matrix .data, sparse .nzval) store exact IEEE representation unaffected. Readers MAY coerce "Float32" native wider float type host language (e.g., double R / C / Python) since IEEE widening exact. String scalars value stored directly JSON string. Examples:
code: scalars/<name>.json {"type":"Float32","value":3.14} {"type":"String","value":"batch_A"}

title: FilesDaf on-disk format
text:
axis single plain-text file: Format (files_format.jl:337–346, files_format.jl:390–393, files_format.jl:974–986): Encoding: UTF-8. One entry per line. line terminated 
 (written via Julia’s println, appends 
 regardless OS). Trailing newline: yes — every entry including last one followed 
. reader splits 
 pops (asserts empty) final element, file must end 
 (files_format.jl:981–983). Forbidden characters: 
 explicitly forbidden axis entry names (files_format.jl:342). characters forbidden format code, though module docstring warns / problematic POSIX filesystems property names used filenames (files_format.jl:75–79). Tabs forbidden. Length: axis length number lines. never stored separately; computed counting lines. Example axes/gene.txt three entries: (line followed 
; file ends 
 MYC.)
code: axes/<axis>.txt BRCA1 TP53 MYC

title: FilesDaf on-disk format
text: Dense non-string vectors stored two files:
code: vectors/<axis>/<name>.json    # metadata descriptor vectors/<axis>/<name>.data    # raw binary payload

title: FilesDaf on-disk format
text: Written write_array_json (files_format.jl:1041–1060): <T> canonical type name Section 4.
code: {"format":"dense","eltype":"<T>"}

title: FilesDaf on-disk format
text: Layout: contiguous sequence elements, one per axis entry, axis order (files_format.jl:447–449). Byte order: little-endian (files_format.jl:111). header, padding. Length: filesize(path) / sizeof(T). axis length axes/<axis>.txt provides expected element count; two must agree (files_format.jl:586–591). Supported element types: StorageReal types — Bool, Int8, Int16, Int32, Int64, UInt8, UInt16, UInt32, UInt64, Float32, Float64 (storage_types.jl:53–81). Bool stored one byte per element (storage_types.jl:31–33: Julia uses Vector{Bool}, BitVector).

title: FilesDaf on-disk format
text:
eltype String format dense, payload file uses .txt extension instead .data (files_format.jl:425–426, files_format.jl:583–585): Format matches axis file: one value per line, UTF-8, 
-separated, trailing 
, 
 forbidden inside values. Length must equal axis length.
code: vectors/<axis>/<name>.txt

title: FilesDaf on-disk format
text: Dense non-string matrices stored :
code: matrices/<rows_axis>/<columns_axis>/<name>.json matrices/<rows_axis>/<columns_axis>/<name>.data

title: FilesDaf on-disk format
text: structure vectors (files_format.jl:655, files_format.jl:683, files_format.jl:1050).
code: {"format":"dense","eltype":"<T>"}

title: FilesDaf on-disk format
text: Layout: column-major (Fortran order) — elements vary fastest along rows axis, along columns axis. Julia’s native Matrix{T} layout column-major; data mmap’d directly Matrix{T} shape (nrows, ncols) (files_format.jl:904–909, files_format.jl:57–58, files_format.jl:111–113). Shape: determined axis lengths, stored file. nrows comes axes/<rows_axis>.txt ncols axes/<columns_axis>.txt. Total expected bytes = nrows * ncols * sizeof(T). Byte order: little-endian. header, padding. Supported element types: dense vectors.

title: FilesDaf on-disk format
text:
eltype String format dense, payload uses .txt (files_format.jl:659, files_format.jl:751–759): Values written column-major order (outer loop columns, inner loop rows, files_format.jl:752–758), one value per line, UTF-8, 
-terminated, trailing 
, 
 forbidden inside values. Total line count must equal nrows * ncols.
code: matrices/<rows_axis>/<columns_axis>/<name>.txt

title: FilesDaf on-disk format
text: Sparse real-valued vectors stored two three files:
code: vectors/<axis>/<name>.json     # descriptor vectors/<axis>/<name>.nzind    # indices of non-zero entries (binary) vectors/<axis>/<name>.nzval    # values of non-zero entries (binary, may be absent for Bool)

title: FilesDaf on-disk format
text: Written write_array_json (files_format.jl:1052–1054). <T> element type; <> index type.
code: {"format":"sparse","eltype":"<T>","indtype":"<I>"}

title: FilesDaf on-disk format
text: Element type: <>, either UInt32 UInt64. UInt32 axis length ≤ typemax(UInt32) (~4.3 billion entries). UInt64 otherwise. Index base: 1-based (Julia-native). Values stored 1-based positions axis entry vector. numeric sparse vectors, data comes directly Julia’s SparseVector.nzind, also 1-based. Byte order: little-endian, contiguous, header. nnz determination: div(filesize(nzind_path), sizeof()) — derived file size, stored separately (files_format.jl:601).

title: FilesDaf on-disk format
text: Element type: <T> (JSON descriptor). Element count: .nzind (nnz). May absent eltype Bool stored non-zero values true. reader synthesises fill(true, nnz) (files_format.jl:615–619). Writers omit file eltype == Bool (nzval(vector)) (files_format.jl:437–439). Byte order: little-endian, contiguous, header.

title: FilesDaf on-disk format
text: eltype String sparse threshold met, .nzval replaced .nztxt text file (files_format.jl:480–496): Contains non-empty string values, one per line, \n-terminated, order corresponding entries .nzind. .nzind file still present holds 1-based positions non-empty values. Sparse threshold string vectors (files_format.jl:476–479): sparse format chosen dense sparse_size ≤ dense_size * 0.75, sparse_size = nonempty_bytes + n_nonempty * (1 + sizeof()) dense_size = nonempty_bytes + total_entries.
code: vectors/<axis>/<name>.nztxt

title: FilesDaf on-disk format
text: Sparse real-valued matrices use Julia’s SparseMatrixCSC layout stored across three files:
code: matrices/<rows_axis>/<columns_axis>/<name>.json    # descriptor matrices/<rows_axis>/<columns_axis>/<name>.colptr  # column pointers (binary) matrices/<rows_axis>/<columns_axis>/<name>.rowval  # row indices (binary) matrices/<rows_axis>/<columns_axis>/<name>.nzval   # values (binary, may be absent for Bool)

title: FilesDaf on-disk format
text: (files_format.jl:664–669, files_format.jl:1052–1054)
code: {"format":"sparse","eltype":"<T>","indtype":"<I>"}

title: FilesDaf on-disk format
text: Element count: ncols + 1. Element type: <> (UInt32 UInt64). Semantics: standard CSC column pointer array. colptr[j] 1-based index first non-zero entry column j within rowval / nzval; colptr[ncols+1] = nnz + 1. values 1-based (files_format.jl:730, files_format.jl:742, files_format.jl:920). nnz determination: computed rowval file size: div(filesize(rowval_path), sizeof()) (files_format.jl:923). Byte order: little-endian, contiguous, header.

title: FilesDaf on-disk format
text: Element type: <>. Element count: nnz. Semantics: 1-based row index non-zero entry, grouped column dictated colptr (files_format.jl:924, files_format.jl:731–738). Byte order: little-endian, contiguous, header.

title: FilesDaf on-disk format
text: Element type: <T>. Element count: nnz. May absent Bool element type values true. Writer omits file eltype(matrix) == Bool && (nzval(matrix)) (files_format.jl:673–675); reader synthesises fill(true, nnz) (files_format.jl:946–950). Byte order: little-endian, contiguous, header.

title: FilesDaf on-disk format
text: rule sparse vectors: UInt32 matrix “size” satisfies max(nrows, ncols, nnz) ≤ typemax(UInt32), UInt64 otherwise (writers.jl:879). descriptor’s indtype field authoritative source; readers must use regardless current axis sizes.

title: FilesDaf on-disk format
text: eltype String sparse threshold met, .nzval replaced .nztxt (files_format.jl:721–748): Contains non-empty values column-major order (outer loop columns, inner loop rows), one per line. .colptr .rowval files present 1-based usual. Sparse threshold string matrices (files_format.jl:718–720): sparse_size = nonempty_bytes + n_nonempty + (ncols + 1 + n_nonempty) * sizeof() vs dense_size = nonempty_bytes + nrows * ncols; sparse chosen ratio ≤ 0.75.
code: matrices/<rows_axis>/<columns_axis>/<name>.nztxt

title: FilesDaf on-disk format
text: version counter files disk FilesDaf format. version counters (axis_version_counter, vector_version_counter, matrix_version_counter) -memory , stored format.internal.version_counters (Dict) within process (formats.jl:1158–1170). persisted across process restarts part -disk specification. reader opening FilesDaf store starts version counters 0. version-related -disk artifact daf.json format version array, encodes format spec version, mutation counter.

title: FilesDaf on-disk format
text: FilesDaf format provides atomic-write crash-recovery guarantees filesystem level. Writes performed : Opening target file open(path, "w") writing content (files_format.jl:280, files_format.jl:339, files_format.jl:447–449). writing entire files via write(path, data) binary arrays (files_format.jl:436–439, files_format.jl:671–675). use .tmp sibling files atomic rename (mv). explicit fsync call. crash mid-write may leave partially-written file. Concurrent-writer safety handled entirely -process via Julia read / write locks (Formats.has_data_write_lock), filesystem-level mechanisms. Multi-process concurrent writes FilesDaf store safe. Single-writer contract. FilesDaf format provides filesystem-level atomicity. writer MUST complete every file write associated property (descriptor + payload) reader opens store, one writer may touch given store time. Multi-process multi-threaded concurrent writers require external coordination (e.g., filesystem lock root directory). Readers MAY observe partially-written store crash mid-write; recovery restoration backup, -format rollback. External writers (outside reference implementations) MUST follow rule: property considered “written” files — descriptor JSON plus binary payload(s) — present disk.

title: FilesDaf on-disk format
text: capitalised (writer-canonical) lowercase (reader-accepted alias) spellings listed. DTYPE_BY_NAME table (operations.jl:216–242) maps type. Int / int alias. Writers emit "Int" "int": Julia (Int == Int64 64-bit systems) dafr produce explicit-width names (Int32, Int64). Readers encountering "Int" / "int" MUST deserialise 64-bit signed integers compatibility hand-written stores legacy pre-spec writers.

title: Native performance and mmap readers
text: dafr pure R + C++ port Julia DataAxesFormats.jl package. Compared Julia-facade wrapper, native : JuliaCall copy tax cross-language boundaries. mmap-backed reads vectors sparse matrices (double-buffer). OpenMP-parallel query kernels (Sum, Mean, Var, Mode, Quantile, …). User-extensible op registry (register_eltwise, register_reduction).

title: Native performance and mmap readers
text: FilesDaf opened read-, dense vectors three payload arrays sparse matrix (colptr, rowval, nzval) mmap’d — OS maps -disk files directly process memory copy. Low-level mmap constructors also exported advanced uses: mmap_dgCMatrix, mmap_int, mmap_lgl, mmap_real.
code: fd <- files_daf("/path/to/daf", mode = "r") x  <- get_vector(fd, "cell", "donor")     # mmap'd — no allocation m  <- get_matrix(fd, "cell", "gene", "UMIs")  # mmap'd dgCMatrix

title: Native performance and mmap readers
text: Reductions size threshold dispatch OpenMP. threshold controlled via options(dafr.kernel_threshold = N) N minimum element count parallel execution. Thread count honours set_num_threads() (dafr.num_threads option). CRAN-like environments auto-capped 2 cores package load.
code: options(dafr.kernel_threshold = 1e5)   # default 1e6

title: Native performance and mmap readers
text: package benchmarked reference Julia implementation (DataAxesFormats.jl) — native Julia -process, R wrapper layer — using shared fixture set. Fixtures hash-match across runners, queries driven catalog, iteration invalidates per-query cache timings reflect real compute rather cache-lookup ceiling. Setup (2026-04-22): dafr commit d6d9a14, R 4.4.1 MKL 2024.1 (ILP64), OMP_NUM_THREADS=1; Julia 1.12.5, OpenBLAS-64, threads = default. Linux x86_64. 79 queries across four fixtures: big_sparse (dense kernel sweeps large CSC matrix), cells_daf (51 queries covering every phrase string DSL), chain_triple (read paths three-layer chain), mmap_reopen (filesystem open + single read). Reading tables. every table Ratio = dafr / julia (lower better dafr) Speedup = julia / dafr (higher better dafr). speedup 10× means dafr ten times faster Julia. Headline. 79 benchmarked queries, dafr faster Julia 56, parity 12, slower 11 (4 exceed 1.5× threshold — filesystem-open--immediately-read micro-test). heavy workloads dominate real pipelines (sparse-matrix reductions, grouped reductions), dafr wins 2×–37×.

what: Heavy kernels on a large sparse matrix
title: Native performance and mmap readers
text: DSL-driven reductions CSC matrix (big_sparse, 10 000 × 10 000, 5 % density, ~5 M nonzeros). runners measure full query path — parse + axis lookup + kernel + result wrap — per-query cache invalidated iteration. OMP_NUM_THREADS=1, threading play. ’s actually compared. wall-time numbers full DSL query, underlying linear algebra. packages wrap query parser, axis-resolution layer, result-wrapping layer around numeric kernel. Profiling shows visible time fixture wrappers, kernel — e.g. get_query(daf, "@ row @ col :: value >- Sum") DataAxesFormats.jl spends ~99.9 % 652 ms framework code; underlying sum(::SparseMatrixCSC; dims=1) returns ~0.5 ms. dafr’s analogous path ~4× leaner per query, dafr also far tight C++ kernel needs absolute terms — runners pay substantial per-query tax. wins therefore two distinct sources, depending row table: Column reductions (*_col, ~4×). Pure framework win. Raw Julia sum(; dims=1) underlying SparseMatrixCSC actually much faster dafr’s column-sum kernel; DataAxesFormats.jl forfeits fast path routing every reduction generic per-column mapfoldl uniformity across operations. 4× gap cost generic path, kernel-quality difference. “Cheap” row reductions CSC (sum_row, mean_row, max_row, var_row, std_row; 15–37×). story, amplified. SparseArrays.jl specialized single-pass dims=2 path outperforms dafr’s C++ kernel head--head; , DataAxesFormats.jl’s generic reduction wrapper use , wrapper happens especially expensive direction. apparent dafr advantage DataAxesFormats.jl framework cost, dafr kernel advantage. “Hard” row reductions CSC (median_row, mode_row, quantile_row, geomean_row; 7–18×). Genuine kernel win. SparseArrays.jl specialized dims=2 path , obvious idiomatic Julia approach (mapslices(median, ; dims=2)) densifies every row — alone takes ~3.5 s allocates ~1.5 GiB fixture. dafr ships hand-rolled CSC-row kernels (kernel_quantile_csc, kernel_mode_csc, kernel_geomean_csc) scan nonzeros without densifying. part table dafr’s kernel , rather framework, source speedup. takeaway: dafr faster DSL runtime DataAxesFormats.jl every row table, (*_col plus cheap row reductions) underlying linear algebra either package already fast. dafr’s specialized C++ reduction kernels matter mostly harder row-wise statistics sparse data (median/mode/quantile/geomean).

what: Open + first-read latency (only place dafr loses)
title: Native performance and mmap readers
text: mmap_reopen fixture — open FilesDaf immediately read one property — dafr consistently slower Julia. four within milliseconds absolute, per-call overhead higher: gap per-call: JSON descriptor parsing, stats, one-shot mmap setup cost per file R Julia. workloads open store stream many reads (common case), cost amortises zero — see kernel tables . open-heavy workflows, keep FilesDaf handle alive across queries.

title: Native performance and mmap readers
text: bake-harness lives benchmarks/ (R runner) benchmarks/julia/ (Julia runner). runners consume query catalog fixture set emit CSVs identical schemas comparison step simple join. See benchmarks/README.md full workflow.

title: Native performance and mmap readers
text: reorder_axes(daf, axis = perm, ...) permutes entries one axes place, rewriting every vector matrix depends axis. accepts named integer permutations (new_entries[] = old_entries[perm[]]): cost bounded data: dependent vector / matrix rewritten exactly via vectorised R indexing (memory-bound). 1k-cell, 1k-gene store ~10 dense vectors 2 dense matrices, full cell-axis reorder runs tens milliseconds single core. files_daf, operation crash-recoverable. -place writes, original tree hardlink-snapshotted .reorder.backup/. process killed mid-reorder, next writable open (mode = "r+" "w+") automatically rolls back backup returning daf caller. Manual recovery also exposed via reset_reorder_axes(daf) rarely needed. reorder_axes() operates leaf storage formats (memory_daf, files_daf, zarr_daf, http_daf — though HTTP read-). Wrappers (chain_reader, chain_writer, view, contractor) rejected front; reorder via underlying writer instead.
code: d <- memory_daf("demo") add_axis(d, "cell", c("c1", "c2", "c3")) set_vector(d, "cell", "score", c(10, 20, 30)) reorder_axes(d, cell = c(3L, 1L, 2L)) axis_vector(d, "cell")     # c("c3", "c1", "c2") #> [1] "c3" "c1" "c2" get_vector(d, "cell", "score")  # 30, 10, 20 #> c3 c1 c2  #> 30 10 20

title: Query DSL
text: Every query two surface syntaxes compile plan: String DSL - terse, good ad-hoc work scripts. Builder pipe-chains - composable R objects; good query fragments computed runtime. canonical_query() parse_query() let inspect string compiles :
code: # String: head(d["@ cell : donor"]) #> demux_07_12_20_1_AACAAGATCCATTTCA-1 demux_07_12_20_1_AACGAAAGTCCAATCA-1  #>                               "N89"                               "N84"  #> demux_07_12_20_1_AAGACAAAGTTCCGTA-1 demux_07_12_20_1_AGACTCATCTATTGTC-1  #>                               "N86"                               "N84"  #> demux_07_12_20_1_AGATAGACATTCCTCG-1 demux_07_12_20_1_ATCGTAGTCCAGTGCG-1  #>                               "N89"                               "N89" # Builder: head(d[Axis("cell") |> LookupVector("donor")]) #> demux_07_12_20_1_AACAAGATCCATTTCA-1 demux_07_12_20_1_AACGAAAGTCCAATCA-1  #>                               "N89"                               "N84"  #> demux_07_12_20_1_AAGACAAAGTTCCGTA-1 demux_07_12_20_1_AGACTCATCTATTGTC-1  #>                               "N86"                               "N84"  #> demux_07_12_20_1_AGATAGACATTCCTCG-1 demux_07_12_20_1_ATCGTAGTCCAGTGCG-1  #>                               "N89"                               "N89" canonical_query("@ cell : donor") #> [1] "@ cell : donor"

title: Query DSL
text: smallest queries look single piece data. keyed many axes push onto stack property.
code: # Scalar (zero axes): d[". organism"] #> [1] "human" # Vector (one axis): head(d["@ donor : age"]) #> N16 N17 N18 N59 N79 N83  #>  61  73  71  75  66  42 # Matrix (two axes): dim(d["@ cell @ gene :: UMIs"]) #> [1] 856 683

title: Query DSL
text: Wrap comparison [ ... ] (string) BeginMask(...) / EndMask() (builder) keep entries pass: Masks compose & (), | (), ^ (XOR), optional ! negation. Combinators left--right, precedence - parenthesize via nested [ ... ] blocks need grouping.
code: # Donors older than 60 - string and builder are equivalent: length(d["@ donor [ age > 60 ]"]) #> [1] 59 length(d[Axis("donor") |> BeginMask("age") |> IsGreater(60) |> EndMask()]) #> [1] 59 # Female donors over 60: length(d["@ donor [ age > 60 & sex = female ]"]) #> [1] 30 length(d[Axis("donor") |>     BeginMask("age") |> IsGreater(60) |>     AndMask("sex") |> IsEqual("female") |>     EndMask()]) #> [1] 30

title: Query DSL
text: Log, Abs, Clamp, Round, Convert, etc. transform values place. string DSL follow % prefix.

title: Query DSL
text: reduction collapses one dimension. matrices, >- reduces row (across column axis), >| reduces column (across row axis); builder forms ReduceToRow() ReduceToColumn(). vectors reduction >> yields scalar.
code: # Per-gene mean UMIs (reduce across cells): head(d["@ gene @ cell :: UMIs >- Mean"]) #> demux_07_12_20_1_AACAAGATCCATTTCA-1 demux_07_12_20_1_AACGAAAGTCCAATCA-1  #>                            3.351391                            4.535871  #> demux_07_12_20_1_AAGACAAAGTTCCGTA-1 demux_07_12_20_1_AGACTCATCTATTGTC-1  #>                            2.411420                            4.131772  #> demux_07_12_20_1_AGATAGACATTCCTCG-1 demux_07_12_20_1_ATCGTAGTCCAGTGCG-1  #>                            2.641288                            3.929722 head(d[Axis("gene") |> Axis("cell") |> LookupMatrix("UMIs") |> ReduceToRow(Mean())]) #> demux_07_12_20_1_AACAAGATCCATTTCA-1 demux_07_12_20_1_AACGAAAGTCCAATCA-1  #>                            3.351391                            4.535871  #> demux_07_12_20_1_AAGACAAAGTTCCGTA-1 demux_07_12_20_1_AGACTCATCTATTGTC-1  #>                            2.411420                            4.131772  #> demux_07_12_20_1_AGATAGACATTCCTCG-1 demux_07_12_20_1_ATCGTAGTCCAGTGCG-1  #>                            2.641288                            3.929722 # Library size per cell (reduce across genes): head(d["@ cell @ gene :: UMIs >| Sum"]) #> demux_07_12_20_1_AACAAGATCCATTTCA-1 demux_07_12_20_1_AACGAAAGTCCAATCA-1  #>                                2289                                3098  #> demux_07_12_20_1_AAGACAAAGTTCCGTA-1 demux_07_12_20_1_AGACTCATCTATTGTC-1  #>                                1647                                2822  #> demux_07_12_20_1_AGATAGACATTCCTCG-1 demux_07_12_20_1_ATCGTAGTCCAGTGCG-1  #>                                1804                                2684 # Vector -> scalar: d["@ donor : age >> Mean"] #> [1] 64.41053

title: Query DSL
text: / vector groups another property length applies reduction per group. -/ / |/ matrix rows / columns.
code: # Mean donor age, split by sex: d["@ donor : age / sex >> Mean"] #>   female     male  #> 63.19231 65.88372 d[Axis("donor") |> LookupVector("age") |> GroupBy("sex", AsAxis()) |> Mean()] #>   female     male  #> 63.19231 65.88372 # Mean UMIs per donor, per gene (group cell rows by their donor): m <- d["@ cell @ gene :: UMIs -/ donor >- Mean"] dim(m) #> [1]  95 683 m[1:3, 1:3] #>         RPL22     PARK7      ENO1 #> N100 6.857143 0.3571429 0.8571429 #> N101 6.375000 0.4375000 1.1875000 #> N102 3.857143 0.2857143 0.4285714

title: Query DSL
text: IfMissing() lets vector / matrix lookup fall back default property doesn’t exist. Useful queries survive across dafs slightly different schemas.
code: head(d[Axis("gene") |> LookupVector("absent_property") |> IfMissing(-1)]) #>  RPL22  PARK7   ENO1  PRDM2 HP1BP3  CDC42  #>     -1     -1     -1     -1     -1     -1

title: Query DSL
text: Use parse_query(s) see operator list string compiles ; easiest way learn grammar analogy. Builder fragments ordinary R objects: store variables, build loops, pass around. expression Axis("cell") |> LookupVector("donor") DafrQuery can index later. ?Axis, ?LookupVector, ?Mean, ?GroupBy (etc.) full per-operator detail; reference grouping also pkgdown “Query builders” section.

title: Query DSL reference
text: query sequence operators executed one operator time. Instead, parser groups operators phrases, phrase stack-rewriting step: query state stack, starting empty. phrase matches pattern top stack (example, LookupVector phrase needs axis specification top). phrase pops matching top elements, performs operation, pushes zero new elements. operators consumed, stack must hold exactly one result; result query returns. grouping--phrases means operator’s meaning depends phrase participates . table operators given , phrase column indicates phrase operator appears .

title: Query DSL reference
text: Element-wise operation builders include Abs, Clamp, Convert, Fraction, Log, Round, Significant. Reduction operation builders include Sum, Mean, Median, Mode, Quantile, Max, Min, Count, GeoMean, Std, StdN, Var, VarN.

title: Query DSL reference
text: Every query returns one four result kinds, selected phrases uses.

title: Query DSL reference
text: Returns set names point data hierarchy.
code: cells[". ?"] #> [1] "organism"  "reference" cells["@ gene : ?"] #> [1] "is_lateral" cells["@ cell @ gene :: ?"] #> [1] "UMIs"

title: Query DSL reference
text: Returns single scalar value. Produced : Looking scalar (. name), optionally || default optional type T pin default’s dtype. Looking vector picking one entry (: vec @ axis = entry). Looking matrix picking one cell (:: m @ rows = R @ cols = C). Reducing vector scalar (... >> Reduction). Reducing matrix scalar (... >> Reduction).
code: cells[". organism"] #> [1] "human" cells[". nope || fallback"] #> [1] "fallback" cells[". nope || 0 type Int64"] #> integer64 #> [1] 0  # Pick one entry from a vector. cells["@ donor : age @ donor = N89"] #> [1] 55  # Number of genes marked as lateral (reduce a vector of Bool). cells["@ gene : is_lateral >> Sum type Int64"] #> [1] 438  # Total UMIs in the whole matrix. cells["@ cell @ gene :: UMIs >> Sum type Int64"] #> [1] 1171936

title: Query DSL reference
text: Returns vector. Phrase kinds: Axis entries: @ axis. Axis entries masking: @ axis [ ... ]. Property lookup along axis: @ axis : property. element-wise op appended: @ axis : property % Op. Reduction matrix along one dimension: @ rows @ cols :: m >- Op (row result) >| Op (column result).
code: head(cells["@ experiment"]) #>   demux_01_02_21_1   demux_01_02_21_2   demux_01_03_21_1   demux_04_01_21_1  #> "demux_01_02_21_1" "demux_01_02_21_2" "demux_01_03_21_1" "demux_04_01_21_1"  #>   demux_04_01_21_2   demux_07_03_21_1  #> "demux_04_01_21_2" "demux_07_03_21_1" length(cells["@ gene [ ! is_lateral ]"]) #> [1] 245 head(cells["@ cell : donor"]) #> demux_07_12_20_1_AACAAGATCCATTTCA-1 demux_07_12_20_1_AACGAAAGTCCAATCA-1  #>                               "N89"                               "N84"  #> demux_07_12_20_1_AAGACAAAGTTCCGTA-1 demux_07_12_20_1_AGACTCATCTATTGTC-1  #>                               "N86"                               "N84"  #> demux_07_12_20_1_AGATAGACATTCCTCG-1 demux_07_12_20_1_ATCGTAGTCCAGTGCG-1  #>                               "N89"                               "N89" # Per-row (per-gene) mean UMIs. head(cells["@ gene @ cell :: UMIs >- Mean"]) #> demux_07_12_20_1_AACAAGATCCATTTCA-1 demux_07_12_20_1_AACGAAAGTCCAATCA-1  #>                            3.351391                            4.535871  #> demux_07_12_20_1_AAGACAAAGTTCCGTA-1 demux_07_12_20_1_AGACTCATCTATTGTC-1  #>                            2.411420                            4.131772  #> demux_07_12_20_1_AGATAGACATTCCTCG-1 demux_07_12_20_1_ATCGTAGTCCAGTGCG-1  #>                            2.641288                            3.929722

title: Query DSL reference
text: Returns matrix. Phrase kinds: Lookup: @ rows @ cols :: property. Count value-combinations: @ axis : v1 * v2. followed element-wise op, row / column grouping + reduction.
code: dim(cells["@ cell @ gene :: UMIs"]) #> [1] 856 683  # Log-transform a dense matrix. dim(cells["@ cell @ gene :: UMIs % Log base 2 eps 1"]) #> [1] 856 683  # Group matrix rows by donor, then reduce each group to its mean. dim(cells["@ cell @ gene :: UMIs -/ donor >- Mean"]) #> [1]  95 683

title: Query DSL reference
text: mask filters axis entries predicate holds. Phrase structure: @ axis [ <mask-body> ]. mask body starts property lookup (BeginMask) followed zero comparisons mask combinators. Supported comparisons: <, <=, =, !=, >=, >, ~ (regex match), !~ (regex non-match). Mask combinators: &, & !, |, | !, ^, ^ !. evaluated left right — grouped precedence. Use separate masks explicit combinators order matters.
code: # Donors older than 30. length(cells[Axis("donor") |>              BeginMask("age") |> IsGreater(30) |> EndMask()]) #> [1] 93  # Same in string form. length(cells["@ donor [ age > 30 ]"]) #> [1] 93  # Negated mask: genes that are NOT lateral. length(cells["@ gene [ ! is_lateral ]"]) #> [1] 245  # Combined masks: age > 60 AND sex = male (on donor axis). length(cells["@ donor [ age > 60 & sex = male ]"]) #> [1] 29

title: Query DSL reference
text: Two operators supply fallback values: || value (IfMissing) — used thing looked exist, reducing empty vector / matrix. ?? value (IfNot) — used chained lookups (: =@ : b) intermediate key empty.
code: cells[". nope || fallback"] #> [1] "fallback"  # Fallback when a chained lookup runs out of pivot values. head(metacells["@ cell : metacell =@ ?? none : type"]) #> demux_07_12_20_1_AACAAGATCCATTTCA-1 demux_07_12_20_1_AACGAAAGTCCAATCA-1  #>                          "MEBEMP-E"                              "none"  #> demux_07_12_20_1_AAGACAAAGTTCCGTA-1 demux_07_12_20_1_AGACTCATCTATTGTC-1  #>                          "MEBEMP-E"                               "MPP"  #> demux_07_12_20_1_AGATAGACATTCCTCG-1 demux_07_12_20_1_ATCGTAGTCCAGTGCG-1  #>                          "MEBEMP-E"                              "none"

title: Query DSL reference
text: Element-wise ops applied %; reductions matrix >- (reduce row) >| (reduce column). take keyword-style sub-arguments separated spaces: Built-element-wise: Abs, Clamp low h, Convert type T, Fraction, Log base b eps e, Round, Significant digits n. Built-reductions: Sum, Mean, Median, Mode, Quantile p P, Max, Min, Count, GeoMean, Std, StdN, Var, VarN. User code can extend either set:
code: daf["... % Log base 2 eps 1e-5"] daf["... >- Mean"] daf["... >| Sum type Int64"] register_eltwise("MySquare", function(x) x * x) register_reduction("MyMax", function(x) max(x, na.rm = TRUE))

title: Query DSL reference
text: Grouping appears three phrase contexts: GroupBy (/) — reduce vector groups defined another vector equal length, yielding shorter vector keyed group. GroupRowsBy (-/) — group rows matrix emit one row per group. GroupColumnsBy (|/) — group columns matrix emit one column per group. matrix-group forms must paired compatible reduction (-/ pairs >-, |/ >|). Appending =@ group property changes result ordered axis’s entries (rather unique group value). Appending || default reduction supplies value empty groups.
code: # Mean per (donor, gene) — collapse cells into donor rows. dim(cells["@ cell @ gene :: UMIs -/ donor >- Mean"]) #> [1]  95 683

title: Query DSL reference
text: =@ declares set values interpreted entries named axis. lets query walk one axis another: , : metacell =@ says “values entries metacell axis”, : type looks type property axis. ?? NA fallback covers cells without assigned metacell. SquareColumnIs (@|) SquareRowIs (@-) slice square matrix specific column / row axis; right-hand side value matrix’s square axis.
code: # Cell -> metacell -> type: pipe values through the metacell axis. head(metacells["@ cell : metacell =@ ?? none : type"]) #> demux_07_12_20_1_AACAAGATCCATTTCA-1 demux_07_12_20_1_AACGAAAGTCCAATCA-1  #>                          "MEBEMP-E"                              "none"  #> demux_07_12_20_1_AAGACAAAGTTCCGTA-1 demux_07_12_20_1_AGACTCATCTATTGTC-1  #>                          "MEBEMP-E"                               "MPP"  #> demux_07_12_20_1_AGATAGACATTCCTCG-1 demux_07_12_20_1_ATCGTAGTCCAGTGCG-1  #>                          "MEBEMP-E"                              "none"

title: Query DSL reference
text: parse_query(s) returns builder AST; canonical_query(q) returns normalised string; forms query produce AST. Inspection helpers: has_query(daf, q) — TRUE q evaluated daf. is_axis_query(q) — TRUE result axis. query_axis_name(q) — axis name (applicable). query_result_dimensions(q) — 0 (scalar), 1 (vector), 2 (matrix). query_requires_relayout(q) — whether matrix relayout needed.
code: s     <- "@ gene : is_lateral" q_bld <- Axis("gene") |> LookupVector("is_lateral") identical(canonical_query(s), as.character(q_bld)) #> [1] TRUE

title: Query DSL reference
text: String literal values inside query can include spaces punctuation. Characters collide DSL’s tokens (@, :, |, [, ], …) must escaped. Use escape_value() produce safe literal unescape_value() reverse .
code: escape_value("a | b") #> [1] "a\\ \\|\\ b"

title: Query DSL reference
text: get_query(daf, q) caches result QueryData inside daf’s internal cache. Use daf[q] one-shot evaluation cache. empty_cache(daf) releases cached results also invoked automatically every mutating write daf cached results never become stale.

title: Query DSL reference
text: Query DSL tutorial — short introduction. Reference: Query DSL (string form) — per-function docs parsing, inspection, escape helpers. Reference: Query builders — per-function docs builder. Upstream spec DataAxesFormats.jl/src/queries.jl semantic cross-reference.

title: Zarr and HTTP storage
text: zarr_daf(path, mode) reads writes Zarr v2 group tree local filesystem. path conventionally ends .daf.zarr: directory ZarrDaf store normal Zarr group. Python consumers can open zarr.open():
code: path <- tempfile(fileext = ".daf.zarr") d <- zarr_daf(path, mode = "w") add_axis(d, "cell", c("c1", "c2", "c3")) set_scalar(d, "organism", "human") set_vector(d, "cell", "score", c(1.5, 2.5, 3.5)) set_matrix(d, "cell", "cell", "kin",            matrix(c(1, 0, 0, 0, 1, 0, 0, 0, 1), 3, 3)) rm(d); gc() #>           used  (Mb) gc trigger  (Mb) max used  (Mb) #> Ncells 2522118 134.7    5071360 270.9  3279762 175.2 #> Vcells 4332980  33.1   10146329  77.5  7201048  55.0  # Reopen read-only and inspect. d <- zarr_daf(path, mode = "r") cat(description(d)) #> name: file29932acb6c0b.daf.zarr #> type: ZarrDaf #> path: /tmp/RtmpleDNeh/file29932acb6c0b.daf.zarr #> mode: r #> scalars: #>   organism: "human" #> axes: #>   cell: 3 entries #> vectors: #>   cell: #>     score #> matrices: #>   cell,cell: #>     kin # Python import zarr g = zarr.open("/path/to/foo.daf.zarr", mode="r") g["axes/cell"][...]          # array(['c1', 'c2', 'c3'], ...) g["scalars/organism"][...]   # array(['human'], dtype=object)

title: Zarr and HTTP storage
text: shipping daf single artifact, zarr_daf(path, mode) accepts .daf.zarr.zip path. store backed mmap’d ZIP archive: reads stored (uncompressed) entries zero-copy ALTREP views mapped region, writes append-commit crash-safe two-phase protocol. Note: single-file .daf.zarr.zip backend depends POSIX mmap available Windows build. Use directory layout Windows. Foreign consumers open archive via Python’s zarr.storage.ZipStore:
code: zip_path <- tempfile(fileext = ".daf.zarr.zip") d <- zarr_daf(zip_path, mode = "w") add_axis(d, "gene", c("g1", "g2")) set_vector(d, "gene", "is_marker", c(TRUE, FALSE)) rm(d); gc() #>           used  (Mb) gc trigger  (Mb) max used  (Mb) #> Ncells 2747243 146.8    5071360 270.9  3279762 175.2 #> Vcells 4707989  36.0   10146329  77.5  7201048  55.0  # Reopen. d <- zarr_daf(zip_path, mode = "r") get_vector(d, "gene", "is_marker") #>    g1    g2  #>  TRUE FALSE # Python import zarr from zarr.storage import ZipStore g = zarr.open(ZipStore("foo.daf.zarr.zip", mode="r"), mode="r")

what: HttpDaf and HttpStore — reading over HTTP(S)
title: Zarr and HTTP storage
text: dafr provides two read-HTTP backends: http_daf(url) — read files_daf directory served HTTP. client downloads metadata.zip open time, parses memory, serves JSON metadata . Non-JSON payloads (axis .txt files, vector / matrix .data etc.) fetched lazily first access via one HTTP GET , cached standard cache layer. zarr_daf("http(s)://...") — read .daf.zarr directory served HTTP. Routes HttpStore, HTTP-backed implementation dafr’s internal ZarrStore interface. Fetches .zmetadata discover tree, pulls chunks demand. backends read-; writable modes hard-error. Server data assumed stable daf open — reopen pick changes. HTTP GET uses 30-second timeout, overridable via options(dafr.http_timeout = N) env DAFR_HTTP_TIMEOUT. automatic retry; flaky-network handling caller’s responsibility. publish files_daf HTTP, directory needs metadata.zip bundle root. dafr 0.2.0 onward maintained automatically every write; pre-0.2.0 stores call pack_files_daf_metadata(path) publishing. zarr_daf("https://...zip") intentionally supported — open zip locally instead, since byte-range reads remote zip need separate handling.
code: # Open a remote files_daf: d <- http_daf("https://example.com/path/to/foo.daf/") get_scalar(d, "organism")  # Open a remote ZarrDaf: d <- zarr_daf("https://example.com/path/to/foo.daf.zarr/", mode = "r") get_vector(d, "cell", "score")  # open_daf() routes by URL pattern: d <- open_daf("https://example.com/path/to/foo.daf.zarr/", mode = "r") # One-time: bundle a pre-0.2.0 store's metadata so HttpDaf clients can # open it. Idempotent; no-op once metadata.zip exists. pack_files_daf_metadata("/srv/www/data/foo.daf")

title: Zarr and HTTP storage
text: Zarr layouts HTTP backend continuously cross-checked zarr-python package test suite. Round-trip parity verified axes, dense sparse vectors, dense sparse matrices, scalars every dafr type. See tests/testthat/test-zarr-python.R, tests/testthat/test-mmap-zip-store-foreign.R, tests/testthat/test-http-live.R live test scripts.

what: Data in Axes in Formats (DAF) for R
title: Data in Axes in Formats (DAF) for R
text: dafr native R + C++ implementation DataAxesFormats (DAF) data model: uniform, self-describing container 1D 2D data arranged along set named axes. Think generalisation AnnData scalars, per-axis vectors, per-axis-pair matrices, disk-backed persistence, composable query DSL. package pure R + C++ port Julia reference implementation - Julia install required.

title: Data in Axes in Formats (DAF) for R
text: package Julia dependency. pure R + C++.
code: # Install from GitHub: remotes::install_github("tanaylab/dafr")  # Or from a local checkout: # R CMD INSTALL /path/to/dafr

title: Data in Axes in Formats (DAF) for R
text: Julia install required - pure R + C++ (JuliaCall copy tax). Mmap-backed reads via mmap_dgCMatrix / mmap_int / mmap_lgl / mmap_real. OpenMP-parallel kernels Sum / Mean / Var / Mode / Quantile / GeoMean. register_eltwise / register_reduction user-defined query ops. Pipe-chain builders: daf[Axis("cell") |> LookupVector("age") |> IsGreater(2)]. AnnData interop via DafAnnData + h5ad_as_daf / daf_as_h5ad.

title: Data in Axes in Formats (DAF) for R
code: library(dafr) #>  #> Attaching package: 'dafr' #> The following object is masked from 'package:graphics': #>  #>     Axis  # Create a DAF object in memory daf <- memory_daf("example")  # Add axes add_axis(daf, "obs", c("cell1", "cell2", "cell3")) add_axis(daf, "var", c("gene1", "gene2"))  # Add vector data set_vector(daf, "obs", "score", c(0.1, 0.5, 0.9))  # Add matrix data mat <- matrix(c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6), nrow = 3, ncol = 2) set_matrix(daf, "obs", "var", "counts", mat)  # Access data get_vector(daf, "obs", "score") #> cell1 cell2 cell3  #>   0.1   0.5   0.9 get_matrix(daf, "obs", "var", "counts") #>       gene1 gene2 #> cell1     1     4 #> cell2     2     5 #> cell3     3     6  # Get a dataframe view get_dataframe(daf, "obs") #>       score #> cell1   0.1 #> cell2   0.5 #> cell3   0.9

title: Data in Axes in Formats (DAF) for R
text: dafr provides two equivalent query interfaces: compact string DSL pipe-composable builders. [ operator DafReader accepts either form. query syntax follows simple pattern: @ axis selects axis . property retrieves scalar : property retrieves vector property :: property retrieves matrix property % operation applies element-wise operation >> reduction applies reduction operation [ property comparison value ] filters property See vignette("queries", package = "dafr") practical tour vignette("query-dsl-reference", package = "dafr") full operator grammar.
code: # Create a sample dataset daf <- memory_daf("query_examples") add_axis(daf, "cell", c("A", "B", "C")) add_axis(daf, "gene", c("X", "Y", "Z")) set_vector(daf, "cell", "age", c(10, 50, 70)) set_vector(daf, "cell", "type", c("T", "B", "NK")) set_scalar(daf, "version", "1.0") set_matrix(daf, "cell", "gene", "counts", matrix(1:9, nrow = 3, ncol = 3))  # Scalar daf[". version"] #> [1] "1.0"  # Axes daf["@ ?"] #> [1] "cell" "gene" daf["@ cell : ?"] #> [1] "age"  "type"  # Vector data daf["@ cell : age"] #>  A  B  C  #> 10 50 70  # Matrix data daf["@ cell @ gene :: counts"] #>   X Y Z #> A 1 4 7 #> B 2 5 8 #> C 3 6 9  # Filter axis by property daf["@ cell [ age > 20 ]"] #>   B   C  #> "B" "C"  # Transform with eltwise op daf["@ cell : age % Abs"] #>  A  B  C  #> 10 50 70  # Pipe-chain builder form daf[Axis("cell") |> LookupVector("age")] #>  A  B  C  #> 10 50 70  # Composed builder: mask + vector lookup daf[     Axis("cell") |>         BeginMask("age") |>         IsGreater(20) |>         EndMask() ] #>   B   C  #> "B" "C"

title: Data in Axes in Formats (DAF) for R
text: Daf object can exposed AnnData-shaped facade familiar X / obs / var / layers / uns / obs_names / var_names / shape properties: facade read-; writes (ann$X <- ...) raise error. Modify underlying Daf directly need change data. Round-trip h5ad /O supported (requires hdf5r Suggests dep). Sparse X, categorical obs / var columns, nested uns, obsm / varm round-trip:
code: d <- example_cells_daf() ann <- as_anndata(d)  ann$n_obs #> [1] 856 ann$n_vars #> [1] 683 dim(ann$X) #> [1] 856 683 head(ann$obs_names) #> [1] "demux_07_12_20_1_AACAAGATCCATTTCA-1" "demux_07_12_20_1_AACGAAAGTCCAATCA-1" #> [3] "demux_07_12_20_1_AAGACAAAGTTCCGTA-1" "demux_07_12_20_1_AGACTCATCTATTGTC-1" #> [5] "demux_07_12_20_1_AGATAGACATTCCTCG-1" "demux_07_12_20_1_ATCGTAGTCCAGTGCG-1" head(ann$obs) #>                                     donor       experiment #> demux_07_12_20_1_AACAAGATCCATTTCA-1   N89 demux_07_12_20_1 #> demux_07_12_20_1_AACGAAAGTCCAATCA-1   N84 demux_07_12_20_1 #> demux_07_12_20_1_AAGACAAAGTTCCGTA-1   N86 demux_07_12_20_1 #> demux_07_12_20_1_AGACTCATCTATTGTC-1   N84 demux_07_12_20_1 #> demux_07_12_20_1_AGATAGACATTCCTCG-1   N89 demux_07_12_20_1 #> demux_07_12_20_1_ATCGTAGTCCAGTGCG-1   N89 demux_07_12_20_1 d  <- h5ad_as_daf("path/to/file.h5ad") daf_as_h5ad(d, "out.h5ad", overwrite = TRUE)

title: Data in Axes in Formats (DAF) for R
text: tbl(daf, axis) returns lazy daf_axis_tbl rows axis entries columns per-axis vectors. dplyr verbs work natively: Supported verbs: filter, select, mutate, arrange, summarise, group_by / ungroup, distinct, pull, collect, as_tibble, rename / relocate, transmute, reframe, slice slice_head / slice_tail / slice_min / slice_max / slice_sample family, count / tally / add_count / add_tally. Inside mutate() / summarise(): window functions (lag, lead, cumsum, row_number, rank family), scalar helpers (if_else, case_when, coalesce, n_distinct, first / last / nth), across((...), ...), tidyselect helpers select(). .= (dplyr 1.1+) works filter / mutate / summarise. See vignette("dplyr", package = "dafr") details. grouping variable names another axis already daf, summarise() count() auto-tie-back lazy daf_axis_tbl axis - pipeline can continue. Axis entries appear name column (convention axis-identity column). Example: Mutations accumulated lazily can written back dplyr::compute(tbl, vectors = c(...)): Matrices scope - use native query DSL .
code: library(dplyr)  d <- example_cells_daf()  tbl(d, "cell") |>     filter(donor == "N89") |>     select(name, donor, experiment) |>     collect() |>     head() #> # A tibble: 6 x 3 #>                                  name donor       experiment #> 1 demux_07_12_20_1_AACAAGATCCATTTCA-1   N89 demux_07_12_20_1 #> 2 demux_07_12_20_1_AGATAGACATTCCTCG-1   N89 demux_07_12_20_1 #> 3 demux_07_12_20_1_ATCGTAGTCCAGTGCG-1   N89 demux_07_12_20_1 #> 4 demux_07_12_20_1_CACAGGCGTCCTACAA-1   N89 demux_07_12_20_1 #> 5 demux_07_12_20_1_CCTACGTAGCCAACCC-1   N89 demux_07_12_20_1 #> 6 demux_07_12_20_1_GAAGGGTGTCCCTGAG-1   N89 demux_07_12_20_1  tbl(d, "cell") |>     count(experiment, sort = TRUE) |>     collect() |>     head() #> # A tibble: 6 x 2 #>               name  n #> 1 demux_28_12_20_2 72 #> 2 demux_28_12_20_1 58 #> 3 demux_21_02_21_2 51 #> 4 demux_22_02_21_2 47 #> 5 demux_01_03_21_1 45 #> 6 demux_07_12_20_2 42 # `donor` is an axis of example_cells_daf(); count() re-anchors onto it. tbl(d, "cell") |>     count(donor, sort = TRUE) |>     class()   # daf_axis_tbl + tbl_df #> [1] "daf_axis_tbl" # Lazy pipeline... updated <- tbl(d, "cell") |>     mutate(donor_upper = toupper(donor))  # ...materialise the new vector on the underlying daf: dplyr::compute(updated, vectors = "donor_upper")

title: Data in Axes in Formats (DAF) for R
text: Full Daf data model: scalars, per-axis vectors, per-axis-pair matrices. Storage backends: memory_daf (-memory), files_daf (one-file-per-property + mmap reads), zarr_daf (directory single-file .daf.zarr.zip, Zarr v2 layout readable zarr-python), http_daf (read-HTTP). Zero-copy mmap-backed reads vectors sparse matrices read-FilesDaf MmapZipStore-backed ZarrDaf. OpenMP-parallel reduction kernels (Sum, Mean, Var, Mode, Quantile, GeoMean). Views adapters zero-copy subsetting / renaming. Chaining repositories efficient overlay / reuse data. Contracts explicit computation input / output validation. Explicit support grouped axes group helpers. Query DSL (string form + pipe-chain builders) user-extensible ops (register_eltwise, register_reduction). AnnData (h5ad) import / export, plus read-AnnData-shaped facade (as_anndata). dplyr backend per-axis tibbles. Control data layout support dense sparse matrices.

title: Data in Axes in Formats (DAF) for R
text: Pre-CRAN, lifecycle: experimental. data model -disk formats stable cross-checked DataAxesFormats.jl zarr-python; R API may still see breaking refinements first CRAN release. See NEWS.md release notes. Comments, bug reports, PRs welcome.

title: Contract-enforcing daf wrapper — ContractDaf
text: S7 class returned contractor() enforcement enabled. Intercepts format_* reads/writes underlying DafReader track access reject operations outside contract.

title: Contract-enforcing daf wrapper — ContractDaf
code: ContractDaf(   name = character(0),   internal = new.env(parent = emptyenv()),   cache = new.env(parent = emptyenv()),   axis_version_counter = new.env(parent = emptyenv()),   vector_version_counter = new.env(parent = emptyenv()),   matrix_version_counter = new.env(parent = emptyenv()),   computation = character(0),   is_relaxed = logical(0),   overwrite = logical(0),   base = DafReader(),   axes = new.env(parent = emptyenv()),   data = new.env(parent = emptyenv()),   tensor_index = new.env(parent = emptyenv()) )

title: Contract-enforcing daf wrapper — ContractDaf
text: name Human-readable identifier Daf store. internal Internal per-store environment used format backends stash backend-specific state; reserved package use. cache Three-tier cache environment (mapped / memory / query). See new_cache_env(). axis_version_counter Environment tracking per-axis mutation counters; invalidates cached reads axis modified. vector_version_counter Environment tracking per-vector mutation counters. matrix_version_counter Environment tracking per-matrix mutation counters. computation Name computation guarded. is_relaxed TRUE, accesses properties outside contract allowed (matching Contract(is_relaxed = TRUE)). overwrite TRUE, pre-existing CreatedOutput properties allowed. base Underlying DafReader / DafWriter. axes Per-axis access-tracking environment (keyed axis name). data Per-property access-tracking environment (keyed <kind>:<axes>:<name>). tensor_index Environment mapping <rows_axis>:<columns_axis>:<mat_name> → tensor key data, used resolve per-entry matrix reads owning tensor tracker.

title: Contract-enforcing daf wrapper — ContractDaf
text: S7 class object; users normally obtain instances via contractor() rather calling constructor directly.

title: Contract-enforcing daf wrapper — ContractDaf
code: withr::with_options(list(dafr.enforce_contracts = TRUE), {     d <- memory_daf()     set_scalar(d, "organism", "human")     ct <- create_contract(scalars = list(         contract_scalar("organism", RequiredInput, "character", "species")     ))     guarded <- contractor("demo_comp", ct, d)     inherits(guarded, "dafr::ContractDaf") }) #> [1] TRUE

title: Read-only HTTP-served FilesDaf. — HttpDaf
text: Daf reader fetches files_daf() directory served HTTP(S). server must expose FilesDaf tree verbatim include metadata.zip bundle root (see pack_files_daf_metadata()). dafr 0.2.0 onward, FilesDaf writes maintain metadata.zip automatically; pre-0.2.0 stores call pack_files_daf_metadata() publishing.

See new_cache_env(). axis_version_counter Environment tracking per-axis mutation counters; invalidates cached reads axis modified. vector_version_counter Environment tracking per-vector mutation counters. matrix_version_counter Environment tracking per-matrix mutation counters. url HTTP(S) URL pointing FilesDaf root.

title: Read-only HTTP-served FilesDaf. — HttpDaf
text: client downloads metadata.zip open time, parses memory, serves JSON metadata (HTTP traffic format_has_* / format_*_set / scalar reads). Non-JSON payloads (axis .txt files, vector/matrix .data / .nzind / .nzval / .colptr / .rowval / .nztxt) fetched lazily first access via one HTTP GET , cached standard cache layer. Read-— mutations supported. Server data assumed stable HttpDaf open; reopen pick changes.

title: Read-only HTTP-served FilesDaf. — HttpDaf
text: GET uses 30-second timeout. Override via options(dafr.http_timeout = N) (seconds) environment variable DAFR_HTTP_TIMEOUT slow links large lazy payloads. Requests issued without retry; flaky-network handling caller.

title: Read-only HTTP-served FilesDaf. — HttpDaf
text: neither name name scalar daf set, default name basename(url). Two HttpDafs opened different hosts basename collide name; pass explicit name disambiguate.

what: Read-only HTTP-served Zarr v3 store. — HttpStore
title: Read-only HTTP-served Zarr v3 store. — HttpStore
text: Implements ZarrStore interface HTTP(S). Used zarr_daf() given http(s):// URL pointing .daf.zarr directory served static web server. Reads Zarr v3 inline consolidated metadata root zarr.json open: v3 stores carry consolidated_metadata.metadata (dict keyed node path) root group instead v2 .zmetadata file. Subsequent store_exists store_list resolve index, node zarr.json reads served (re-serialized bytes reader otherwise fetched per node), chunk fetches cached process memory.

title: Read-only HTTP-served Zarr v3 store. — HttpStore
code: HttpStore(   url = character(0),   index = list(),   chunk_cache = new.env(parent = emptyenv()) )

title: Read-only HTTP-served Zarr v3 store. — HttpStore
text: url Root URL served Zarr v3 directory. index Named list: v3 node metadata index parsed root zarr.json consolidated_metadata.metadata (keyed node path, /zarr.json suffix). chunk_cache Environment caching fetched chunk bytes (root zarr.json) path.

title: Read-only HTTP-served Zarr v3 store. — HttpStore
text: Writes hard-error: HTTP-served Zarr read-. Zarr version: backend targets Zarr v3 -disk layout dafr reads writes locally (DataAxesFormats.jl 0.3.0). legacy v2 store (root .zmetadata, root zarr.json) rejected open python -m zarr v2_to_v3 conversion hint, mirroring local DirStore / MmapZipStore v2 rejection. Note distinct http_daf() (FilesFormat--HTTP path http_daf()), uses metadata.zip unaffected.

title: Zarr Store abstract base class. — ZarrStore
text: root (DirStore) Filesystem root path; created missing. consolidate_cache (DirStore/DictStore) Internal environment holding -memory consolidated-metadata index, maintained incrementally across writes set_* need re-parse whole store root every mutation. Created automatically; intended direct use. env (DictStore) Internal environment used key-value backing store; typically created new_dict_store(). path (MmapZipStore) Filesystem path zip archive. mode (MmapZipStore) One "r", "r+", "w+", "w"; set new_mmap_zip_store(). xptr (MmapZipStore) Internal external pointer C++ store; set new_mmap_zip_store() intended direct use.

what: Apply a computation to a renaming view of a DafWriter. — adapter
title: Apply a computation to a renaming view of a DafWriter. — adapter
text: Mirrors Julia adapter(computation, daf; input_axes, input_data, capture, output_axes, output_data, empty, relayout, overwrite, name). typical use run @computation (R: computation()-wrapped) function whose expected property names differ names stored daf.

title: Apply a computation to a renaming view of a DafWriter. — adapter
code: adapter(   daf,   fn,   input_axes = NULL,   input_data = NULL,   output_axes = NULL,   output_data = NULL,   capture = memory_daf,   empty = NULL,   relayout = TRUE,   overwrite = FALSE,   name = ".adapter" )

title: Apply a computation to a renaming view of a DafWriter. — adapter
text: daf DafWriter — base data read write . fn function taking single DafWriter argument (adapted chain). Return value passes . input_axes, input_data Passed viewer() input view. least one output_axes / output_data must non-NULL (otherwise adapter() degenerates fn(daf)). output_axes, output_data Passed viewer() output view. capture Factory function returning fresh DafWriter. Default memory_daf. empty Named list list("<axis>|<vector>" = default, "<r>|<c>|<m>" = default) supplying default values entries present daf's axis absent source view's. NULL (default) disables feature. relayout TRUE (default), matrix copies also write transposed layout. overwrite TRUE, pre-existing destination entries replaced. name Human-readable name input/capture/adapted dafs. Default ".adapter".

title: Apply a computation to a renaming view of a DafWriter. — adapter
text: return value fn(adapted).

title: Apply a computation to a renaming view of a DafWriter. — adapter
text: Flow: input = viewer(daf, axes = input_axes, data = input_data) exposes subset computation consumes, possibly renamed axes / names. capture = capture_factory(name = "<base>.capture") fresh writable. adapted = chain_writer(list(input, capture)) — reads fall input, writes go capture. result = fn(adapted) — computation's return value. output = viewer(adapted, axes = output_axes, data = output_data) — selects + renames outputs. Copy output daf via internal helper. Honors overwrite, relayout, empty. Return result.

title: Apply a computation to a renaming view of a DafWriter. — adapter

axis_vector(adapted, "obs")         set_vector(adapted, "obs", "rank", seq_along(entries))     },     input_axes  = list(list("obs", "@ cell"), list("cell", NULL)),     input_data  = VIEW_ALL_VECTORS,     output_axes = list(list("cell", "@ obs"), list("obs", NULL)),     output_data = list(list(ALL_VECTORS, NULL), list(c("cell", "rank"), "=")) ) #> <dafr::WriteChainDaf> #>  @ name                  : title: Cache-group constants. — cache_group_constants
text: Character scalar.

title: Cache-group constants. — cache_group_constants
text: MEMORY_DATA — value -memory R object; cache "memory" tier (subject LRU + memory cap). MAPPED_DATA — value mmap-backed view (zero-copy); cache "mapped" tier (uncapped, LRU). QUERY_DATA — value query-evaluation intermediate; cache "query" tier (subject LRU + memory cap). names mirror upstream DataAxesFormats.jl's CacheGroup enum. runtime tier names ("mapped" / "memory" / "query") accept constant form lowercase form via empty_cache().

what: Wrap a function so a contract is enforced on every call. — computation
title: Wrap a function so a contract is enforced on every call. — computation
text: Returns closure , call, wraps first (daf) argument contractor(), runs verify_input(), executes fn, runs verify_output(), returns fn's result. contract enforcement disabled (env DAF_ENFORCE_CONTRACTS / option dafr.enforce_contracts falsy), wrapping still performed verification -op, wrapper cheap leave place.

title: Wrap a function so a contract is enforced on every call. — computation
code: computation(name, contract, fn)

title: Wrap a function so a contract is enforced on every call. — computation
text: name Human-readable computation name (character scalar). Used contract violation messages suffix wrapper's daf name. contract Contract() describing inputs outputs. fn function wrap. Must accept DafReader/DafWriter first argument.

title: Wrap a function so a contract is enforced on every call. — computation
text: function signature fn. bound contract name attached attributes (dafr_contract, dafr_computation_name).

title: Wrap a function so a contract is enforced on every call. — computation
text: Scope: single contract (matching Julia's exported @computation Contract form). Two-contract / three-contract variants (Julia UNTESTED) supported; pass single Contract use merged one via merge_contracts.

title: Wrap a function so a contract is enforced on every call. — computation
code: withr::with_options(list(dafr.enforce_contracts = TRUE), {     c <- Contract(         axes = list(cell = list(RequiredInput, "per-cell axis")),         data = list(contract_vector("cell", "donor",             RequiredInput, "character", "donor id"))     )     read_donors <- computation("read_donors", c,         function(daf) get_vector(daf, "cell", "donor"))     d <- memory_daf()     add_axis(d, "cell", c("c1", "c2"))     set_vector(d, "cell", "donor", c("d1", "d2"))     read_donors(d) }) #>   c1   c2  #> "d1" "d2"

code: concatenate(   destination,   axis,   sources,   names = NULL,   dataset_axis = "dataset",   dataset_property = TRUE,   prefix = FALSE,   prefixed = NULL,   empty = NULL,   merge = NULL,   sparse_if_saves_storage_fraction = 0.25,   overwrite = FALSE )

title: Concatenate multiple dafs along one or more axes. — concatenate
text: destination DafWriter. Must empty concatenation axes. axis single axis name character vector axis names. sources List DafReaders concatenate. names Optional character vector unique data set names (defaults source's name prop). dataset_axis Name per-source axis create. NULL disables. dataset_property TRUE (default) dataset_axis non-NULL, create -named vector every concatenation axis holding source name entry. prefix Logical (single per-axis). Prefix concat-axis entries "<dataset_name>." de-duplicate across sources. prefixed Optional character vector (list vectors per axis) additional property names prefix, beyond heuristic (-name "<axis>.*" properties). empty Named list fill values missing per-source properties. merge Named list mapping property keys merge action ("SkipProperty", "LastValue", "CollectAxis"). sparse_if_saves_storage_fraction Numeric (default 0.25); reserved future sparse-promotion heuristic. overwrite TRUE, allow replacing pre-existing destination entries.

title: Wrap a daf with a contract for a computation — contractor
code: withr::with_options(list(dafr.enforce_contracts = TRUE), {     c <- Contract(         axes = list(cell = list(RequiredInput, "per-cell axis")),         data = list(contract_vector("cell", "donor",             RequiredInput, "character", "donor id"))     )     d <- memory_daf()     add_axis(d, "cell", c("c1", "c2"))     set_vector(d, "cell", "donor", c("d1", "d2"))     cd <- contractor("demo", c, d)     class(cd)[[1]] }) #> [1] "dafr::ContractDaf"

what: Copy everything from one daf to another. — copy_all
title: Copy everything from one daf to another. — copy_all
text: Mirrors Julia copy_all!(; destination, source, empty, types, overwrite, insist, relayout). Copies order: scalars, axes, vectors, matrices. Tensors auto-expanded — users can call copy_tensor() explicitly per-main-axis-entry matrix groups.

title: Copy everything from one daf to another. — copy_all
code: copy_all(   destination,   source,   empty = NULL,   types = NULL,   overwrite = FALSE,   insist = TRUE,   relayout = TRUE )

title: Copy everything from one daf to another. — copy_all
text: destination DafWriter. source DafReader. empty Named list mapping flat keys fill values: "axis|name" -> value vectors, "rows|cols|name" -> value matrices. Matrix keys accept either axis order. types Named list type-coercion strings flat-key form plus "name" (pipes) scalars. overwrite TRUE, replace pre-existing destination entries. insist TRUE (default) raise pre-existing conflicts overwrite = FALSE; FALSE silently skip. relayout TRUE (default), also write transposed layout copied matrices.

title: Copy everything from one daf to another. — copy_all
text: Invisibly, destination.

title: Copy everything from one daf to another. — copy_all
text: Axes already destination overwritten (regardless overwrite flag). destination axis must identical subset source axis (else empty required per-vector / per-matrix fill missing entries). Unknown--source destination axes left untouched.

title: Copy everything from one daf to another. — copy_all
code: src <- memory_daf(name = "src") set_scalar(src, "organism", "human") add_axis(src, "cell", c("c1", "c2")) set_vector(src, "cell", "age", c(10L, 20L)) dest <- memory_daf(name = "dest") copy_all(dest, src, relayout = FALSE)

what: dafr: Data in Axes in Formats (DAF) for R — dafr-package
title: dafr: Data in Axes in Formats (DAF) for R — dafr-package
text: native R implementation 'DataAxesFormats.jl' https://github.com/tanaylab/DataAxesFormats.jl data model, providing uniform interface accessing 1D 2D data arranged along arbitrary axes. generalizes extends 'AnnData'-like functionality, supporting scalars multiple data modalities (e.g. RNA-seq, ATAC-seq), efficient memory management via memory-mapped reads, composable query DSL. Unlike 'dafJuliaWrapper' https://github.com/tanaylab/dafJuliaWrapper interface, 'dafr' 'Julia' dependency; storage, query evaluation, reductions implemented directly R C++ optional 'OpenMP' parallelism. package ships -memory ('MemoryDaf') file-backed ('FilesDaf') storage, 'AnnData' facade h5ad read/write, computation contracts pre/post-condition validation, 'dplyr' backend tidy-verbs access axis data.

title: dafr: Data in Axes in Formats (DAF) for R — dafr-package

what: Parallel log(x + eps) / log(base) on dense numeric input. — fast_log
title: Parallel log(x + eps) / log(base) on dense numeric input. — fast_log
text: Mirrors fast-path % Log takes inside query evaluator, callable directly -memory numeric matrix vector without wrapping input memory_daf(). Intended downstream packages whose hot paths already matrix materialised R (e.g. session-level cache) want avoid single-threaded log(x + eps, base) query fallback otherwise incur.

title: Parallel log(x + eps) / log(base) on dense numeric input. — fast_log
code: fast_log(   x,   eps = 0,   base = exp(1),   threshold = dafr_opt("dafr.kernel_threshold") )

title: Parallel log(x + eps) / log(base) on dense numeric input. — fast_log
text: x Dense numeric matrix numeric vector. Integers silently promoted double. eps Non-negative scalar added inside log. Default 0. base Positive scalar. Default exp(1). threshold Parallel-threshold: enable OpenMP length(x) >= threshold. Defaults dafr_opt("dafr.kernel_threshold").

title: Parallel log(x + eps) / log(base) on dense numeric input. — fast_log
text: numeric matrix vector matching shape x. Names dimnames preserved.

title: Parallel log(x + eps) / log(base) on dense numeric input. — fast_log
text: base values 2, 10, exp(1) dispatch std::log2, std::log10, std::log respectively — matching R's preference accurate form log(x) / log(base). Result equal log(x + eps, base) machine precision (verified query-julia-compat parity suite).

title: Parallel log(x + eps) / log(base) on dense numeric input. — fast_log
File-backed Daf store. — files_daf
text: Daf store backed directory small self-describing files, bidirectionally compatible Julia's DataAxesFormats.FilesDaf. Writes non-atomic; one writer may touch store time.

title: File-backed Daf store. — files_daf
code: files_daf(path, mode = c("r", "r+", "w", "w+"), name = NULL)

title: File-backed Daf store. — files_daf
text: path Directory path. mode One "r" (read-, store must exist), "r+" (read-write, store must exist), "w" (create; fails store already exists), "w+" (create open existing store; store already exists, scalars, axes, vectors, matrices subdirectories daf.json truncated, unrelated files directory preserved). name Human-readable identifier. Defaults basename(path).

title: File-backed Daf store. — files_daf
text: FilesDaf instance (DafWriter "r+"/"w"/"w+", FilesDafReadOnly/DafReadOnly "r").

title: File-backed Daf store. — files_daf
text: files_daf lock store. Two writers opening path mode "r+" "w+" race metadata.zip rebuilds per-entry JSON writes, guarantee last-writer-wins consistency. supported pattern single-writer plus arbitrary read-readers; cross-process concurrency must coordinated externally (e.g., job scheduler).

title: File-backed Daf store. — files_daf
code: path <- tempfile("dafr-example-") d <- files_daf(path, mode = "w") add_axis(d, "cell", c("c1", "c2")) set_scalar(d, "organism", "human") rm(d) unlink(path, recursive = TRUE)

text: Invisibly destination path.

title: Convert a files_daf directory to a zarr_daf directory. — files_to_zarr
text: -filesystem . function probes hard-link src dst front; link fails (typically paths different filesystems) call errors automatic copy fallback. Note: implementation correctness-first; numeric blobs re-written regular set_* API rather hard-linked. probe still enforces -filesystem constraint future hard-link optimization can ship without API break.

what: Control OpenMP thread count for dafr kernels. — set_num_threads
title: Control OpenMP thread count for dafr kernels. — set_num_threads
text: set_num_threads(n) caps number OpenMP threads used every subsequent kernel dispatch. get_num_threads() reports current cap.

title: Control OpenMP thread count for dafr kernels. — set_num_threads

title: Control OpenMP thread count for dafr kernels. — set_num_threads
text: n Positive integer; maximum number OpenMP threads use. Values < 1 clamped 1.

title: Control OpenMP thread count for dafr kernels. — set_num_threads
text: set_num_threads invisibly returns n. get_num_threads returns current OpenMP max-threads value.

title: Control OpenMP thread count for dafr kernels. — set_num_threads
text: dafr's kernels default system's OpenMP maximum (typically OMP_NUM_THREADS machine's CPU count). CRAN policy allows two cores package checks, .onLoad() detects CRAN-like environments (via _R_CHECK_LIMIT_CORES_ OMP_THREAD_LIMIT ≤ 2) caps 2 automatically. Interactive users can raise lower cap time.

title: Control OpenMP thread count for dafr kernels. — set_num_threads
code: old <- get_num_threads() set_num_threads(1L)   # single-thread mode get_num_threads()     # 1 #> [1] 1 set_num_threads(old)  # restore

title: Per-column bounded top-K with OpenMP. — top_k_per_col

title: Per-column bounded top-K with OpenMP. — top_k_per_col

title: Per-column bounded top-K with OpenMP. — top_k_per_col
text: x Numeric dense matrix, Matrix::dgCMatrix. k Positive integer - number entries return per column. use_abs Rank abs(value) TRUE, else raw value. Values returned always signed. threshold Parallel-threshold: enable OpenMP across columns ncol(x) >= threshold. Defaults dafr_opt("dafr.kernel_threshold").

title: Per-column bounded top-K with OpenMP. — top_k_per_col
text: list two k--ncol matrices: indices Integer matrix row indices (1-based). NA_integer_ padding slots. values Double matrix signed values. NA_real_ padding slots. Column names x preserved output columns; row-name lookup caller's responsibility (use rownames(x)[indices]).

title: Per-column bounded top-K with OpenMP. — top_k_per_col
text: Rows missing valid comparison score (NA / NaN dense input; absent column's nonzero entries CSC input) excluded consideration. fewer k entries qualify, tail output column NA-padded.

title: Zarr store generics. — zarr_store_generics
