title: dafr 0.4.5
text: Parity-audit release: exhaustive differential sweep DataAxesFormats.jl 0.3.0 (55 confirmed divergences triaged) produced fixes . test-driven (failing-first test, minimal fix) full suite stays green. See dev/parity-audit-2026-06-11/ audit trail.

title: dafr 0.4.5
text: Dense /X dense layers use canonical AnnData (n_obs, n_var) orientation. Previously dense matrix written read transpose, producing (n_var, n_obs) -disk /X: real scanpy/anndata file failed load (dimension mismatch) dafr-written h5ad files transposed relative ecosystem. reader now reshapes known axis lengths (robust hdf5r dropping singleton dimension vector), writer emits AnnData encoding attributes (array /X layers; dataframe _index + column-order obs/var) row column names round-trip instead falling back 0..n-1. Sparse /X (explicit shape/indptr/indices) already correct unchanged. Verified directions Python anndata. obsm/varm dense embeddings use canonical (n_axis, k) orientation, fix /X. Per-obs per-var embeddings now interoperate wider AnnData ecosystem.

title: dafr 0.4.5
text: GroupBy/CountBy order group labels bytewise (method = "radix"), matching Julia, instead ambient LC_COLLATE. Group output now locale-independent.

title: dafr 0.4.5
text: concatenate() errors collect-axis source lacks scalar collected, instead silently producing NA.

title: dafr 0.4.5
text: get_vector() errors named default whose names mismatch axis order, rather silently misaligning values. reader API exposes reserved name/index virtual vectors via has_vector()/get_vector(), matching Julia.

title: dafr 0.4.5
text: set_matrix() defaults relayout = TRUE, matching Julia: storing matrix now also stores transposed layout default. behavior/storage-size change; pass relayout = FALSE keep single layout. Internal call sites immediately relayout (example_*_daf(), files/zarr converters) updated default collide.

title: dafr 0.4.5
text: copy_matrix() transpose-reads flipped-source instead erroring. copy_all() copies -layouts matrix (Julia’s columns_axis >= rows_axis guard) instead hitting “existing matrix” collision relayout = TRUE. copy_tensor() empty = NULL skips missing slice instead erroring.

title: dafr 0.4.5
text: reconstruct_axis() rewrites implicit property string foreign key new axis (Julia’s overwrite_implicit_values condition), keeps empties-mapping key properties empties.

title: dafr 0.4.5
text: dense reader reconstructs elided -fill chunk fill_value vector matrix paths, instead reading zeros/garbage writer omits -fill chunk.

title: dafr 0.4.5
text: Adapter copy-back uses insist = TRUE: name collision copy-back now errors instead silently dropping data. computation() threads overwrite contractor, idempotent re-run overwrite = TRUE succeeds (COMP-01).

title: dafr 0.4.5
text: group_names uses FNV-32 hash rather Julia’s simhash (shape-parity ); ~/!~ regex match collects multi-token patterns allow unescaped metacharacters; contracts keep dafr’s stricter Optional/GuaranteedOutput enforcement (safer DAF.jl 0.3.0, raised upstream); relayout_matrix() write-chain succeeds layouts Julia errors. Unsigned Float32 widths UInt64 precision bounded R’s native types (documented; correctness regression).

title: dafr 0.4.4
text: Singleton chains return input unwrapped (C1). chain_reader(list(d)) now delegates read_only(d), chain_writer(list(d)) (explicit name) returns d , read_only() identity data already read-unless name forces fresh wrapper - matching DataAxesFormats.jl chains.jl / read_only.jl. Previously every singleton chain allocated redundant wrapper. concatenate() MERGE_COLLECT_AXIS preserves sparse vectors (M2). collect-axis vector merge now applies Julia’s storage-savings heuristic (sparse_if_saves_storage_fraction, default 0.25): sparse storage save least fraction, collected (axis x dataset) result built Matrix::sparseMatrix instead always materializing dense. Sparse sources contribute nnz, dense sources full length; string bit64 columns stay dense.

title: dafr 0.4.3
text: FilesFormat v1.1 read now pinned real Julia output. Reading DataAxesFormats.jl 0.3.0 v1.1 FilesDaf stores worked since 0.4.0, test real Julia-written v1.1 repo skipped without live Julia env. Added committed Julia-0.3.0-written flat v1.1 fixture (tests/testthat/fixtures/jf11: scalar + dense/sparse vector + dense/sparse matrix) always-test reads - confirming v1.1 interop needs c-blosc/zstd (Julia writes flat default; explicitly blosc-packed components require c-blosc, fails actionable “install c-blosc” message).

title: dafr 0.4.3
text: ZarrDaf axis-name decode now memoized. format_axis_array / axis_vector previously re-decoded vlen-utf8 axis-name strings store every call, every distinct query axis re-paid decode (exact-repeat query hit result cache). 4000+2500 fixture ~45% dense matrix-query’s time. decoded entries now cached "memory" tier keyed axis + axis version stamp (invalidated delete_axis, contract vector/matrix caches), distinct queries axes decode . Measured ~1.6x >| Sum query; % Log axis-touching query benefit identically. Chains/views ZarrDaf inherit cache automatically.

title: dafr 0.4.2
text: Packed FilesDaf read now exercised across every numeric dtype (Int8..UInt64, Float32/64) plus -true Bool sparse, NaN/Inf, vlen-utf8 unicode/control strings (previously Float64/Int64). Vector >> reductions NaN/±Inf inputs pinned DAF.jl. Sparse Float/Bool/Int vector round-trips (memory + files), copy_vector superset empty fill, group_names determinism. Documented divergence: >> Median vector containing NaN ±Inf returns NaN dafr vs -Inf DataAxesFormats.jl (every reduction input NaN ). R’s NaN arguably correct answer; matching Julia require reimplementing median kernel. See dev/adversarial-parity/FINDINGS.md (E1).

title: dafr 0.4.1
text: Shorten committed packed-fixture directory names (tests/testthat/fixtures/{zpk,fpk}/...) every path source tarball stays 100-byte portable limit. R’s internal tar (used R CMD build Windows) emits “storing paths 100 bytes portable” warning , CI’s error-: warning turned failure. functional change.

title: dafr 0.4.0
text: FilesDaf now writes v1.1 -disk format ({"version":[1,1]}), matching DataAxesFormats.jl 0.3.0. change v1.0 sparse property JSON descriptor: now carries per-component object (nzind / nzval vectors, colptr / rowval / nzval matrices), shaped like stand-alone dense descriptor eltype n_elements. binary payload files byte-identical v1.0, reader accepts shapes, existing v1.0 repos still read. Verified round-trip directions DAF 0.3.0 (dafr reads Julia’s v1.1; Julia reads dafr’s).

title: dafr 0.4.0
text: Writing many properties directory/-memory ZarrDaf now near-linear instead O(N^2). set_* updates root consolidated metadata incrementally (editing -memory index node zarr.json strings re-assembling root string concatenation) rather re-scanning re-parsing whole store every mutation. Writing 400 vectors dropped ~45 s ~1.7 s. store stays consistent disk every write (flush/close step), output unchanged.

title: dafr 0.4.0
text: ZarrDaf now reads writes Zarr v3 -disk format used DataAxesFormats.jl 0.3.0 (single zarr.json per node, c/-prefixed chunk keys, daf version marker root-group attribute, inline consolidated metadata). Flat (uncompressed) read write supported. Breaking: legacy Zarr v2 reader/writer removed. Opening Zarr v2 .daf.zarr now errors conversion hint (python -m zarr v2_to_v3), matching DataAxesFormats.jl 0.3.0’s behaviour. ## ZarrDaf: packed/sharded v3 read Reading packed/sharded (packed=true) Zarr v3 .daf.zarr stores now supported (read-; dafr still writes flat). packed array’s start-located shard index (ZEP-0002, crc32c-checked) parsed R, inner chunks decode via gzip (base R, always available) - optional system library present - c-blosc (default blosc_zstd_bitshuffle / blosc_lz4_bitshuffle codecs) libzstd (plain zstd). Dense sparse matrices/vectors, including vlen-utf8 strings, covered; flat sub-threshold components packed store read . CRAN-safe: configure probes c-blosc/libzstd (honouring BLOSC_HOME / ZSTD_HOME / CONDA_PREFIX). neither present flat path unchanged blosc/zstd-packed read raises actionable “install c-blosc/libzstd” error. crc32c always compiled (dependency).

title: dafr 0.4.0
text: Reading packed (chunked + compressed) FilesDaf / HttpDaf properties now supported (read-; dafr still writes flat). packed property dual-format shard (<name>.zip, <name>.<component>.zip independently-packed sparse component) carries start-located Zarr v3 shard index packed ZarrDaf array. dafr reads index, reusing crc32c + gzip / c-blosc / libzstd decode backend (optional-library rules CRAN-safety apply). Dense sparse matrices/vectors vlen-utf8 strings covered; flat sub-threshold components (small colptr, scalars, short vectors) store read . foreign "zipped"-shard (ZIP archive leading Zarr index) rejected actionable message.

what: ZarrDaf over HTTP: Zarr v3 read
title: dafr 0.4.0
text: Reading Zarr v3 .daf.zarr HTTP now works. zarr_daf("http://...") parses v3 inline consolidated metadata root zarr.json (v3 write v2 .zmetadata file) node index, serves node metadata index, fetches chunks lazily HTTP. Scalars, axes, dense sparse vectors, strings, bools, dense sparse matrices round-trip. legacy Zarr v2 store served HTTP rejected python -m zarr v2_to_v3 conversion hint local path. HttpDaf / FilesDaf HTTP (FilesFormat path, http_daf()) unaffected; path use Zarr .

previous_headings: ZarrDaf over HTTP: Zarr v3 read
title: dafr 0.4.0
text: dafr reads packed/sharded v3 stores ever writes flat (common default). Reading blosc/zstd-packed stores needs optional c-blosc / libzstd system libraries (see ); gzip-packed flat stores read extra dependency. Local directory (DirStore), zip (MmapZipStore, .daf.zarr.zip), HTTP v3 stores supported.

what: Fix: read DataAxesFormats.jl 0.3.0 FilesFormat v1.1 directories
title: dafr 0.3.1
text: DataAxesFormats.jl 0.3.0 bumped FilesDaf -disk format 1.0 1.1. binary blobs byte-identical, sparse property’s JSON sidecar moved top-level eltype/indtype keys per-component descriptors (nzind/nzval vectors; colptr/rowval/nzval matrices). dafr 1.0-reader rejected 1.1 directories outright (incompatible format version: 1.1). dafr now: accepts FilesFormat minor version 1 (still writes 1.0, reads 1.0 1.1); parses legacy top-level v1.1 per-component sparse descriptors - deriving element type nzval component (Bool absent) index type index component - mirroring DataAxesFormats.jl’s parse_sparse_descriptor; raises clear error 0.3.0 “packed” (.zip, chunked + compressed) sparse components, yet supported (re-save flat components). HttpDaf (FilesDaf served HTTP) gets treatment: accepts v1.1, parses per-component sparse descriptors, rejects packed components. covers reading flat FilesFormat 1.1 repos (directory HTTP) , rest 0.3.0 (zarr/zip “packed view directory Zarr” machinery).

what: Fix: ZarrDaf on-disk format now interoperates with DataAxesFormats.jl
title: dafr 0.3.1
text: .daf.zarr stores written dafr DataAxesFormats.jl mutually unreadable. Opening Julia-written store R failed missing daf.json; opening R-written store Julia failed daf data set. Three divergences upstream (DataAxesFormats.jl v0.2.0, src/zarr_format.jl) caused , now fixed: daf marker array. Upstream marks store Zarr array named daf holding two UInt8 bytes [MAJOR, MINOR] = [1, 0] validates via haskey(root.arrays, "daf"). dafr wrote plain daf.json file instead. dafr now writes (validates) daf marker array longer writes daf.json Zarr stores. breaking change dafr Zarr -disk format: .daf.zarr stores written earlier dafr versions (carry daf.json, daf array) readable version. FilesDaf daf.json marker unchanged. Intermediate .zgroup markers. Upstream writes real .zgroup every group (four scalars/axes/vectors/matrices containers, eagerly, plus every sub-group). dafr wrote root .zgroup synthesised rest inside consolidated .zmetadata - enough zarr-python’s consolidated reader, Julia’s directory-store open navigates real .zgroup files raised KeyError: key "axes" found. dafr now writes .zgroup every group. Read-side dtype coverage. chunk reader understood <f8/<i4/<i8/|b1/|O. now also reads |u1, <u1, <i1, <u2, <i2, <u4, <u8, <f4 - unsigned, narrow, Float32 dtypes upstream legitimately emits (Float32 expression matrices, unsigned index arrays, |u1 marker ). Dense-matrix chunk separator. dafr wrote multi-dimensional chunk keys / dimension separator (chunk file 0/0); upstream Zarr v2 default use . (chunk file 0.0). Julia reader looked 0.0, find , failed missing chunks fill_value. (Sparse matrices unaffected - components 1-D, whose single chunk 0 either way.) dafr now uses . separator arrays, matching upstream. Round-trip interop directions covered tests/testthat/test-zarr-julia-interop.R (gated dafr-mcview Julia env), zarr-python interop remains green.

title: dafr 0.3.1
text: Rewrote vignette("queries") cover element-wise transforms, compound masks (string + builder), reductions row / column / scalar, GroupBy vectors matrices, IfMissing fallbacks. Previously vignette demonstrated trivial lookups. Expanded vignette("dafr") (Getting Started) description(), dplyr backend demo, working files_daf round-trip, explicit vignette pointers chains / views / contracts / computations. Fixed stale “Limitations (0.1.0)” block vignette("anndata"). Sparse CSR / CSC, categorical columns, dense layers, obsm / varm matrices supported since 0.2.x; vignette now lists actual current gaps (obsp, varp, raw, sparse layer / obsm / varm entries). README: dropped “First public release: 0.1.0” status line, added Zarr HTTP backends Key Features, expanded dplyr verb list, pointed DSL link vignette("query-dsl-reference") instead Julia upstream page.

title: dafr 0.3.1
text: vignette("dafr") persistence example called copy_all(d, fd) destination source swapped. example gated eval = FALSE bug invisible chunk set evaluate. Package code correct; vignette affected.

what: CI: fix stale Round test expectation (test-operations-registry.R:117)
title: dafr 0.2.8.1
text: Round(c(1.44, 1.55), digits = 1) returns c(1.4, 1.6) Float64, default Int64 cast (Julia parity, Round 5/6) rejects InexactError. test written parity fix still expected float result. Updated expect_error("InexactError") plus type = "Float64" assertion keeps fractional-result coverage. Resolves CI break introduced v0.2.8.

what: Fix: set_matrix rejects matrices with mismatched dimnames (Round-7 G7)
title: dafr 0.2.8.1
text: set_matrix(d, "cell", "gene", "UMIs", m) dimnames-bearing matrix m previously discarded rownames(m) / colnames(m) silently overwrote axis entries readback. caller passing typo’d dimnames (e.g. c("X","Y","Z") axis c("","B","C")) saw error. Now set_matrix validates dimnames axis entries raises mismatch: row names : matrix mismatch entry names axis: <> column names : matrix mismatch entry names axis: <> Mirrors Julia data.jl > set_matrix > named > !rows|!columns > name.

what: Tests: port copies.jl > matrix > sparse > {superset, disjoint} grid (Round-7 G8)
title: dafr 0.2.8.1
text: 12 new regression tests covering sparse-matrix copy edge cases Julia exercises: superset rows / cols () (raises), empty = NULL (raises), empty = -1 (fills -1), empty = 0 (fills zero). disjoint rows / cols (), empty = NULL, empty = -1 (raise disjoint entries...). dafr’s copy_matrix already matched Julia semantically; guards lock behaviour . See tests/testthat/test-copies-sparse-grid.R.

what: Query DSL: Julia parity sweep (Round 5 + Round 6)
title: dafr 0.2.8
text: 200 adversarial probes (Round 5) 1000-query grammar fuzzer (Round 6) surfaced long tail silent wrong-answer cases, type contracts diverged DataAxesFormats.jl, error messages drifted alignment. Major user-visible changes: Centralised op-invocation validator. per-op .reject_* dispatches scattered across five eltwise / reduction / grouped-reduction handlers replaced .OP_META + .validate_op_invocation R/op_dispatch.R. type-tag rejection now fires parse time axis / property lookup, mirroring Julia. integer64 demoted eltwise reduction dispatch Float64-kernels see expected type instead bit-aliased doubles. Median preserves NaN like Julia rather promoting NA. Mask comparators strings bytewise "é" < "f" agrees Julia’s lexicographic order. Significant high/low, Round digits, Convert Bool non-{0,1}, Float32 sum Int32, … now raise InexactError instead returning silently-wrong values. Convert Round accept full set dtype aliases (Int8/16/32/64, UInt8/16/32/64, Float32/64, Bool plus lowercase R-style aliases). IfMissing default validated parse time. Typed defaults must -range declared eltype; hex / binary literals named constants pi / e accepted. Latin-1 / Unicode value tokens raise unexpected character error Julia instead parsing corrupted token. Bare ?? without lookup chain raises invalid operation rather returning tautology. GroupBy Bool keys uses lowercase "true" / "false" bucket labels. NaN group key IfMissing fills NaN bucket user default instead dropping . BeginMask eager-rejects properties matrix names error surfaces (expensive) lookup path. Matrix-reduction fast paths honour type = parameter via .cast_to_type; previously type cast silently dropped fast path. % Clamp low/high rejected parse time. (Julia exposes via min / max; Clamp DAF operation.) parameter: X exist operation: Y missing required parameter: X (Significant / Convert / Quantile) expected: value (expected value comparator ...) Comparator--non-string drops comparison operation: X suffix. Round-5 / Round-6 adversarial harness lives dev/adversarial-parity/ (R + Julia runners, Python diff tool, 1100-query corpus, FINDINGS.md per-bug provenance). 55 regression tests added across tests/testthat/test-query-* tests/testthat/test-operations-*.

title: dafr 0.2.8
text: Six bugs surfaced new dev/backend-parity/ audit harness, round-trips 82-item fixture Memory/Files/Zarr write -> reopen -> read every (src, dst) copy_all pair. fixes: 11/246 (single-backend) 35/567 (cross-backend) diverged. : 0/246 0/567. NaN scalars now accepted. set_scalar(d, "x", NaN) previously raised value may NA .assert_scalar_value used .na(value), returns TRUE NaN. NaN valid Float64 per Julia DAF; true NA rejected now. Float64 scalars round-trip full precision FilesDaf. set_scalar(d, "pi_val", pi) used read back 3.1416 jsonlite::toJSON defaulted digits = 4. scalar writer now passes digits = 17. Int64 / UInt64 dense vectors round-trip across full 64-bit range FilesDaf. reader’s readBin(= "integer", size = 8L) silently truncated value low 32 bits (base R 8-byte integer type), values whose low 32 bits zero (2^32, 2^62, -2^62, …) came back 0. reader now reads 8-byte doubles bit-aliases integer64. -NaN Float64 vectors preserve NaN FilesDaf. auto-sparsifier counted NaN zero (sum(vec != 0, na.rm = TRUE) drops NaN), -NaN vector written empty sparse vector read back -zero. NaN now counted nonzero sparsify decision kept sparse representation. ZarrDaf reorders named-subset vectors axis order. set_vector(d, "cell", "x", c(C = 3, = 1, B = 2)) ZarrDaf previously stored values input order; Memory Files stored axis order. .validate_vector_value (performs reorder) now called user-facing set_vector dispatcher every backend - current future - receives axis-ordered, un-named vec. FilesDaf scalar strings declare Encoding() == "UTF-8". regex fast-path .read_scalar_json returned bytes-strings tagged "unknown". byte content always correct (identical() returned TRUE), serialize()-based comparisons distinguished tag. Regression tests live tests/testthat/test-backend-parity-r7.R (one focused case per bug class). audit harness, fixture, findings doc, diff tool live dev/backend-parity/.

what: Fix: row/col mask alignment under matrix GroupBy
title: dafr 0.2.7
text: @ axis [ prop = X ] @ :: M -/ prop >- Op (GroupColumnsBy mirror) returned correct values assigned wrong group label. mask filtered matrix axis correctly, subsequent GroupRowsBy / GroupColumnsBy fetched group-property vector FULL axis length matched masked matrix position - group labels matrix rows drifted alignment. Concrete repro regression test: Julia parity: MatrixState DataAxesFormats.jl keeps per-axis VectorState matrix, masks groupings always aligned axis. R port passing matrix state plain list dropped row/col indices transition; now matrix-lookup carries row_indices/col_indices forward apply_groupby_rows / apply_groupby_columns subset group vector match. Two regression tests added test-query-eval-masks.R (E3 row + cols variants).
code: d <- memory_daf(name = "t") add_axis(d, "metacell", c("M1","M2","M3","M4","M5","M6")) add_axis(d, "gene", c("G1","G2")) add_axis(d, "type", c("A","B","C")) set_vector(d, "metacell", "type", c("A","A","B","B","C","C")) set_matrix(d, "metacell", "gene", "UMIs", matrix(     c(11,12, 21,22, 31,32, 41,42, 51,52, 61,62),     nrow = 6L, byrow = TRUE,     dimnames = list(c("M1","M2","M3","M4","M5","M6"), c("G1","G2"))))  # Pre-fix: returned A=72,74 (which is the M3+M4 sum, mis-labelled). # Post-fix: B=72,74 only. get_query(d, "@ metacell [ type = B ] @ gene :: UMIs -/ type >- Sum")

what: CI: document source = param + refresh pkgdown index
title: dafr 0.2.6
text: R CMD check ---cran platforms flagged WARNINGs v0.2.5 ship; pkgdown also failed sitrep check: man/register_eltwise.Rd / man/register_reduction.Rd new source = NULL parameter (introduced conflict-source capture hook v0.2.5’s CR1 fix) \usage{} matching \item{source} arg block. _pkgdown.yml missing newly-exported register_query_operation() Op registry reference section. regenerated indexed. man pages also re-emitted roxygen 8.0.0 (drops \docType{data} / \format{} / \keyword{datasets} constant exports; otherwise behaviour change).

title: dafr 0.2.5
text: register_query_operation(kind, name, fn) now exposed single user-facing entry point adding custom eltwise / reduction ops dafr query DSL. Mirrors Julia’s register_query_operation() DataAxesFormats.Registry. Collision errors now match Julia’s template: Previously error <name> already registered; use overwrite = TRUE. new wording includes registration source locations (captured automatically caller’s srcref, supplied explicitly via new source = parameter).
code: conflicting registrations for the eltwise operation: <name> first in: <file>:<line> second in: <file>:<line>

title: dafr 0.2.4
text: R CMD check Linux devel / oldrel, Windows, macOS hit code/documentation mismatches v0.2.2 / v0.2.3 ship. Two man pages date code: man/is_leaf.Rd still documented S7-generic signature function(daf, ...); R2 refactor v0.2.2 made is_leaf() plain wrapper function(daf). man/reconstruct_axis.Rd missing properties_defaults parameter added v0.2.3. regenerated. behavioural changes.

what: Concat / contracts / reconstruction parity (M1 + M4 + C1 + CR3)
title: dafr 0.2.3
text: concatenate(merge = list(ALL_SCALARS = ...)) wildcards now expand source properties concat time. Mirrors Julia’s [ALL_SCALARS => action] / [ALL_VECTORS => action] / [ALL_MATRICES => action]. Explicit non-wildcard keys still override wildcard-expanded entries. “can’t collect axis scalar:” error wording now matches Julia byte--byte. concatenate(prefixed = list(axis = c(...))) now override fires regardless prefix[axis]. list names cell-axis vectors whose values reference prefixed axis thus need dataset name spliced . Previously dafr ANDed per-axis prefix flag list, vectors listed prefixed[cell] prefixed prefix[cell] == FALSE. merge_contracts() rejects integer vs character (cross-lattice mixes) Julia’s incompatible type: error. Previously dafr’s type lattice total order (logical < integer < double < character) pair quietly merged narrower type. Now character integer64 siblings numeric chain - -chain merges still pick narrower type, cross-chain merges error. reconstruct_axis(..., properties_defaults = list(prop = val)) merges pre-existing axis. Implicit-property values must subset axis entries; extra entries get per-property default value. Mirrors Julia’s reconstruct_axis!(..., properties_defaults = (; prop = val)).

what: Reorder parity (R2 + R4 + R5 + R6 closed)
title: dafr 0.2.2
text: is_leaf() accepts S7 class objects well instances, mirroring Julia’s is_leaf(::Type{<:DafReader}). Class-level call returns TRUE concrete leaf classes (MemoryDaf, FilesDaf{,ReadOnly}, ZarrDaf{,ReadOnly}, HttpDaf) FALSE abstract DafReader / DafWriter / DafReadOnly. zarr_daf now supports reorder_axes(). Best-effort -place reorder via existing zarr overwrite path. Crash recovery (backup--restore) yet implemented; mid-reorder crash leaves store undefined state. reorder_axes(list(d1, d2), axis = perm) reorders multiple writers one call (Julia: reorder_axes!([d1, d2], Dict(...))). axis must agree entry order across every writer (axis: <> entries differ error otherwise); writers missing axis silently skip . memory_daf reorder now atomic. pre-reorder snapshot parked daf’s internal state; SimulatedCrash mid-reorder rolled back reset_reorder_axes(), now returns TRUE pending reorder rolled back, FALSE otherwise. Mirrors Julia’s reset_reorder_axes! Bool contract.

title: dafr 0.2.0
text: release closes bulk user-facing semantic divergences dafr DataAxesFormats.jl main. R query DSL now mirrors Julia byte--byte operator level; escape_value / unescape_value use Julia’s \<char> backslash convention. Operation parity (CO1-CO7 + CT1/CT3 closed): % Op type=<T> now coerces result. Previously type silently ignored numeric eltwise ops (Abs / Exp / Sqrt / Round / Log / Clamp). Honors integer / numeric / double / Float32/Float64 / Int8-64 / UInt8-64 / Bool / logical. % Log rejects non-positive x + eps Julia’s value must : positive template instead returning NaN. % Fraction rejects integer types (Julia: value must : float type) rejects scalar input Julia’s applying Fraction eltwise operation scalar wording. % Significant accepts low (defaults high = low), matching Julia. % GeoMean error wording aligned Julia’s value must : negative / parameter: eps / operation: GeoMean template. % Abs / % Clamp reject non-numeric type= Julia’s value must : number type template. eltwise / reduction ops use Julia error template: invalid value: "<v>" / value must : <constraint> / parameter: <name> / operation: <op>. Viewer parity (V2-V7 closed): Wildcard * view contracts validates values = NULL (Julia: (*, *), (*, prop), (axis, *) shapes). Scalar-shape validation: vector-producing query scalar slot now errors instead silently exposing vector via get_scalar. Strict-include semantics: passing data = list(...) view_daf() now exposes listed properties; data = NULL retains original “expose ” behaviour. __axis__ placeholder substitution matrix slot queries. Vector-slot rejects matrix-shape queries Julia’s matrix query: ... / vector: ... wording. Naming parity: Grouped reduction results (-/, |/) now use alphabetical group ordering match Julia’s factor(..., levels = sort(unique)), first-appearance order. Recovered fixes: complete_path() returns NULL memory-backed dafs non-FilesDaf chains instead erroring; mirrors Julia’s complete_path returning nothing non-Files dafs. complete_chain() retains explicit error since requires real -disk path. memory_daf accepts Matrix::sparseVector. atomic-validator now detects sparseVector via S4 inheritance; readback densifies name attachment. Storage roundtrip preserves values. Test suite (5579 PASS / 0 FAIL / 142 SKIP, vs 5024 PASS / 0 FAIL / 49 SKIP start parity push): 23 *-jl-parity.R files mirror DataAxesFormats.jl’s 22 main test files (contracts.jl split across 7 sub-slices R tractable ports). remaining 142 skips --scope feature gaps documented per-test (R divergence codes), behavioral divergences: multi-contract @computation, description(deep), empty_* builders, h5df backend, file-bridge, tensors--views, etc.

what: Earlier in 0.2.0: Julia parity for chains, concat, reorder
title: dafr 0.2.0
text: literal port DataAxesFormats.jl::concat.jl, reorder.jl, chains.jl test suites surfaced closed five behavior gaps: concatenate(..., merge = list("axis|name" = MERGE_COLLECT_AXIS)) now honors empty= missing-source columns. Previously, source lacked vector collect-axis-merged, source’s column destination matrix filled NA regardless user’s empty= map. Now empty fill consulted; neither source empty provides value, “empty value” error per-axis vector path raised. concatenate(..., merge = list("rows|cols|name" = MERGE_LAST_VALUE)) now actually fires matrix properties (rows / cols concat set). Previously silent -op — dispatch reached 3-part-key case didn’t write anything. destination now holds last source’s matrix. reorder_axes() now errors missing axes (silent skip). Aligned Julia’s reorder_axes! contract. reset_reorder_axes() now returns invisible(TRUE/FALSE): TRUE pending reorder rolled back, FALSE pending. Mirrors Julia’s Bool return. Existing callers ignored return value () unaffected. ReadOnlyChainDaf / WriteChainDaf version counters now propagate underlying sources. Previously chain wrappers private *_version_counter env never tracked source mutations — vector_version_counter(chain, ...) returned 0 even source-side set_vector bumped source’s counter. importantly, broke cache invalidation chain: set_vector(chain, ..., overwrite = TRUE) (routes write chain’s writer), reads chain returned cached pre-write value instead new value. chain’s stamp / counter functions now sum per-source counters.

title: dafr 0.2.0
text: files_daf() now works Windows. Previously every write failed MmapZipStore supported Windows. Windows, dafr skips optional metadata.zip bundle (used serving FilesDaf HTTP via http_daf()); local reads, writes, round-trips unaffected. pack_files_daf_metadata() errors clear message, zarr_daf() rejects .daf.zarr.zip paths — use unzipped .daf.zarr directory store instead, run Linux/macOS zip-backed storage.

title: dafr 0.2.0
text: get_query() format API now return named values matching Julia NamedVector / NamedMatrix convention: Lookup vectors / matrices carry axis-entry names / dimnames. Axis listings (@ cell, @ donor [ age > 60 ]) return character vectors names == values. IfMissing-default vectors (@ cell : missing || 0 Int64) carry names . behavior change — code expect_equal(get_query(...), unnamed_vec) may need updating expect named results. ALTREP-mmap vectors (mmap_real / mmap_int / mmap_lgl) preserve ALTREP status across names<-, mmap region stays shared rather copied.

title: dafr 0.2.0
text: Closes remaining gaps literal port DataAxesFormats.jl::queries.jl test suite. New query forms supported: Top-level comparators : / :: return boolean vector / matrix: @ cell : score < 1.0, @ cell @ gene :: UMIs > 0. Standalone : / :: IfMissing. : age || 1 @ cell = X returns 1 age missing; matrix form :: UMIs || 0 @ cell = X @ gene = . Implicit AsAxis fallback. @ cell : type.manual : color resolves type axis type.manual axis. Matrix-column slice auto-relayout. @ cell :: UMIs @ gene = works regardless UMIs storage orientation. Cols-axis mask second axis. @ rows @ cols [ filter ] :: M filters cols axis; matrix lookup honours row column filters. Virtual name property every axis. [ name = X ] : name return axis-entry vector. Eltwise scalar. . score % Abs applies element-wise numeric scalars (: '%' eltwise requires vector matrix scope). Regex escapes masks ([ type ~ \^\[-U\] ]). Empty-string round-trip. escape_value("") ''; unescape_value("''") "". Stricter error reporting: Partial queries (@ cell @ gene lookup) error invalid query: <canonical> instead silently returning NULL. second ? Names result also errors. Empty-matrix reductions without IfMissing always error (: output-axis-empty branch silently returned empty vector). Numeric reductions character matrix error non-numeric input instead leaking base R’s 'x' must numeric. ?? sentinel : prop raises clear parse error sentinel can’t coerced lookup vector’s type (: silent NA via R’s .integer warning). Parser errors unknown operations / parameters repeated parameters now match Julia DAF wording. query_axis_name() agrees get_query() compound-mask queries (@ cell [ is_low & UMIs @ gene = B ]).

title: dafr 0.2.0
text: Sum(), Mean(), Median(), Min(), Max(), Mode(), Count(), GeoMean(), Quantile(), Std(), StdN(), Var(), VarN() now produce canonical reduction form (>> Sum) instead % Sum. previous emission element-wise op erred runtime piped matrix vector. Behavior change: stored canonical strings builders change % Sum >> Sum. ReduceToColumn() / ReduceToRow() accept shapes back-compat. canonical_query() also accepts DafrQuery directly. >> Mode / >| Mode now accept character factor inputs, matching Julia operation’s documented support strings. IfMissing defaults vector matrix lookups thread default coercion : age || 1 returns integer column (character one).

title: dafr 0.2.0
text: get_dataframe() get_dataframe_query() accept list mixing positional bare names name = ":query" pairs: Mirrors Julia’s ["age", "doublet" => ":is_doublet"].
code: get_dataframe(d, "cell", columns = list("age", doublet = ":is_doublet"))

title: dafr 0.2.0
text: [ prop < value ], [ prop > value ], etc. property stored factor (e.g. h5ad categorical loaded via categorical encoding) now compare stored strings lexically, matching Julia. Previously returned NA (unordered factor) compared level codes (ordered factor).

title: dafr 0.2.0
text: .Rbuildignore excludes AGENTS.md CLAUDE.md longer surface top-level files NOTE R CMD check.

title: dafr 0.2.0
text: description(daf) now emits per-format header lines (url: HttpDaf, path: + mode: FilesDaf / ZarrDaf) name: / type: lines. New internal format_description_header generic mirrors upstream Formats.format_description_header; default emits just type: <ClassName>, per-format methods extend . New exported is_leaf(daf) predicate. Returns TRUE storage formats state directly (MemoryDaf, FilesDaf / FilesDafReadOnly, ZarrDaf / ZarrDafReadOnly, HttpDaf) FALSE wrappers (ReadOnlyChainDaf, WriteChainDaf, ContractDaf, ViewDaf). Mirrors upstream Readers.is_leaf. reorder_axes() now rejects non-leaf inputs front clear "non-leaf type: <Class> daf data: <name> given reorder_axes" error (previously surfaced cryptic missing-method dispatch). complete_path() now works ZarrDaf (returns directory path, :memory:, zip path, HTTP URL — whichever store path constructor recorded). previously FilesDaf-.

title: dafr 0.2.0
text: New HttpDaf backend read-access FilesDaf directory served HTTP(S). client downloads metadata.zip open serves JSON metadata ; non-JSON payloads (.txt / .data / .nzind / .nzval / .colptr / .rowval / .nztxt) fetched lazily via one HTTP GET . New HttpStore (R/http_store.R) implements R/zarr_store.R store interface HTTP. zarr_daf("https://host/foo.daf.zarr/") routes ; reads .zmetadata serves .zarray/.zattrs/.zgroup . open_daf("https://...") dispatches http_daf zarr_daf based URL suffix. HTTP backends read-; writable modes hard-error. *.daf.zarr.zip URLs explicitly scope redirect users opening underlying .daf.zarr directory. New pack_files_daf_metadata(path) exported helper bundle FilesDaf tree’s JSON metadata metadata.zip (trees written older dafr modified outside dafr). FilesDaf now maintains metadata.zip automatically every set_* / delete_* / add_axis / delete_axis / reorder_axes operation, plus one-shot rebuild writable open bundle missing. Mirrors upstream DataAxesFormats.jl::FilesFormat. Pre-0.2.0 stores picked automatically first time ’re opened mode "r+" "w+". axes/metadata.json sidecar now maintained FilesDaf (sorted JSON array axis names). Required HTTP clients enumerate axes without GET-ing every axes/*.txt. New Imports: httr2 (transitively curl).

title: dafr 0.2.0
text: New MmapZipStore (C++ src/mmap_zip_store.cpp) backs ZarrDaf single ZIP archive local filesystem. open_daf() zarr_daf() now accept .daf.zarr.zip paths return working ZarrDaf / ZarrDafReadOnly. Reads use shared mmap archive: stored (method-0) entries returned zero-copy ALTREP RAW views via new ZipRawAltrep class. Deflate-compressed (method-8) entries decompressed demand via system zlib; deflate64 / methods raise clear error pointing stored / deflate re-save. Writes append entries via upstream’s two-step commit protocol (commit central directory + EOCD first, write local file header data now-sparse hole). Crash-safe: writable open’s recovery pass detects partial commits via tail validation (LFH signature + data CRC32) rolls back trailing run invalid entries returning. Internal tick-counter hooks every commit-able decision point let recovery tested deterministically (5 tick points; tests gated NOT_CRAN=true). Always emits ZIP64 (per upstream DataAxesFormats.jl); every local file header padded 0xDAF1 extra field data region starts 8-byte-aligned file offset (zero-copy unaligned-load safety every host architecture). ALTREP safety net: store closes (GC’d), every outstanding ALTREP vector produced deactivated — length() returns 0 Dataptr() returns stable inert byte. R callers keep references past close get clean empty raws instead segfaults. Internal-dafr:::dafr_mmap_zip_reserve() / dafr:::dafr_mmap_zip_patch_crc() expose two-phase fill large sparse arrays (writable -place ALTREP view + post-fill CRC patch). Crash reserve patch rolls back via CRC-mismatch path ordinary partial commits. SystemRequirements: zlib (linked via -lz). Cross-language smoke: dafr-written .daf.zarr.zip archives open cleanly Python via zipfile zarr.open(zarr.storage.ZipStore(...)). Foreign zips written python -m zipfile (stored deflate) open cleanly dafr. Mirrors DataAxesFormats.jl mmap_zip_store.jl (~1070 LOC Julia ported ~1300 LOC C++ + cpp11 + ALTREP).

title: dafr 0.2.0
text: New zarr_daf(uri, mode, name) backend reading writing Zarr v2. Two store impls: DirStore (filesystem directory tree) DictStore (-memory). Zip-backed Zarr also supported via MmapZipStore backend (see ). New files_to_zarr(src, dst) zarr_to_files(src, dst) conversion helpers (-filesystem ; correctness-first implementation re-encodes public API; hard-link optimization deferred perf follow-). open_daf("foo.daf.zarr") now returns ZarrDaf. Compression policy: dafr writes Zarr chunks uncompressed; reads uncompressed gzip; rejects blosc/zstd/lz4 clear error pointing re-save compressor=None. Sparse layouts mirror upstream DataAxesFormats.jl: 1-based -disk indices nzind / colptr / rowval; sparse-Bool -TRUE skips nzval (storage compaction). Cross-language parity verified via gated Python zarr.open() smoke tests. Mirrors DataAxesFormats.jl v0.2.0 commits ea4b5f9 (Zarr v2 directory tree), 8cc3ff6 (-memory store), 47e7693 (CRC fix — N/-memory layer), 79034fd (.zmetadata consolidation), 46d4ab2 (Files↔︎Zarr conversion).

title: dafr 0.2.0
text: New reorder_axes(daf, axis = perm, ...) permutes axis entries place, rewriting every vector matrix depends axis. files_daf operation crash-recoverable via .reorder.backup/ directory hardlinks; next files_daf(path, mode = "r+" | "w+") open, -progress reorder automatically rolled back pre-reorder state. New reset_reorder_axes(daf) manually trigger recovery (mostly redundant given auto-recovery open). New open_daf(uri, mode, name) factory function — dispatches path / URL pattern. memory:// (path) → memory_daf, filesystem path → files_daf, *.daf.zarr / *.daf.zarr.zip → zarr_daf, http(s):// → http_daf. factory replaces previous filesystem-open_daf R/complete.R. Mirrors DataAxesFormats.jl v0.2.0 commits 90301ff, 070bd34 (axis reordering) b40377f (open_daf factory).

title: dafr 0.2.0
text: internal format API now returns per-item cache classifications, matching DataAxesFormats.jl v0.2.0 (upstream commit 49fbba1). user-visible behavior change. Every backend format_get_* method (scalar/axis_array/vector/matrix) returns list(value, cache_group) instead bare value. Every backend format_set_* method returns cache_group constant just-written value (NULL) instead invisible(). New exported character constants MEMORY_DATA, MAPPED_DATA, QUERY_DATA — accepted empty_cache(daf, clear = ...) / keep = ... alongside existing lowercase forms. reader-level cache (R/readers.R) now consults backend-returned cache_group storing fresh reads, instead hardcoding "memory" tier. mmap-eligible reads files_daf now correctly land "mapped" tier. Per-item classification: files_daf returns MEMORY_DATA string/factor reads (R’s CHARSXP cache makes mmap moot strings) MAPPED_DATA everything else. Matches upstream’s structural classification — size thresholds. refactor preparatory ZarrDaf HttpDaf backends, require per-item classification drive internal caching.

title: dafr 0.1.0 (development)
text: Three DataAxesFormats.jl query-DSL features previously missing implemented, closing last semantic gaps dafr upstream Julia package: >> Reduction reduces vector matrix scalar (e.g. @ gene : is_lateral >> Sum type Int64, @ cell @ gene :: UMIs >> Sum). >> longer silently aliased >|; grouped input (... / g >> Sum) continues produce per-group vector, . @ axis = entry picks one entry vector (@ cell : age @ cell = N89) one cell matrix (@ cell @ gene :: UMIs @ cell = C @ gene = X). Two successive picks collapse matrix scalar. || value type T attaches Julia-style dtype (Bool, Int8..Int64, UInt8..UInt64, Float32, Float64, String) scalar-lookup default, matching existing behaviour suffix inside reductions element-wise ops. IfMissing() builder gains optional type kwarg (IfMissing(0, type = "Int64")).

title: dafr 0.1.0
text: First public release. native R + C++ implementation DataAxesFormats (DAF) data model multi-dimensional data along arbitrary axes, ported Julia reference implementation Julia dependency.

title: dafr 0.1.0
text: Core model. Scalars, per-axis vectors, per-axis-pair matrices, axis entries, cache invalidation. memory_daf() files_daf() backends. Query DSL. String form (daf["@ cell : donor"]) pipe-chain builders (daf[Axis("cell") |> LookupVector("donor") |> IsGreater(2)]). Memory-mapped reads vectors sparse matrices read-files_daf() store (zero-copy). OpenMP-parallel C++ kernels Sum / Mean / Var / Mode / Quantile / GeoMean, CRAN-compliant 2-core auto-cap via set_num_threads(). AnnData interop. DafAnnData R6 facade, as_anndata(), h5ad_as_daf(), daf_as_h5ad() — sparse X, categorical obs/var, nested uns, obsm/varm round-trip. dplyr backend. tbl(daf, axis) → lazy daf_axis_tbl filter, select, mutate, arrange, summarise, group_by, distinct, pull, collect, compute (write-back). Contracts. create_contract(), verify_contract(), contract_scalar() / contract_vector() / contract_matrix() / tensor_contract() / axis_contract() computation pre/post-condition validation. See pkgdown site full documentation.
